1ah|"""
2ps|Base configuration primitives shared by all config sections.
3rd|
4cy|Provides the :class:`BaseAppConfig` class used as the parent for all
5yb|domain-specific configuration models.
6jc|"""
7th|
8kt|from __future__ import annotations
9th|
10eo|from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
11th|
12th|
13qh|class BaseAppConfig(BaseSettings):
14qn|    """Root configuration base class.
15th|
16pq|    All domain-specific config classes inherit from this base to share
17wi|    the ``CDL_LLM_`` environment variable prefix and ``.env`` file
18lt|    loading behaviour.
19th|
20ov|    Attributes:
21mq|        model_config: Pydantic Settings configuration controlling env
22yz|            variable parsing.
23ck|    """
24th|
25sx|    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
26pr|        env_prefix="CDL_LLM_",
27uv|        env_file=".env",
28re|        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
29dy|        case_sensitive=False,
30oh|        extra="ignore",
31ds|    )
32nd|
33rd|
34ps|__all__ = ["BaseAppConfig"]
35th|

1ah|"""
2xs|cdl_llm — Internal LLM platform library.
3rd|
4ck|Provides production-grade, vendor-independent abstractions for:
5th|
6jt|- **LLM backends** — OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama (``cdl_llm.llm``)
7qu|- **Embeddings** — OpenAI, HuggingFace local (``cdl_llm.embeddings``)
8jt|- **Vector stores** — Qdrant, Chroma, PGVector (``cdl_llm.vectorstore``)
9kx|- **Chunking** — Fixed, recursive, semantic, markdown, code (``cdl_llm.chunking``)
10xr|- **Prompts** — Versioned Jinja2 templates with registry (``cdl_llm.prompts``)
11pb|- **Output parsers** — JSON, structured, with retry (``cdl_llm.output``)
12nh|- **Memory** — In-memory, Redis, Postgres (``cdl_llm.memory``)
13oa|- **Gateway** — Router, fallback chain, rate limiter, semantic cache (``cdl_llm.gateway``)
14go|- **Agents** — ReAct, tool-calling, supervisor (``cdl_llm.agents``)
15yn|- **Tools** — Web search, SQL, HTTP, file reader (``cdl_llm.tools``)
16wx|- **LangGraph templates** — Pipeline, supervisor, reflection, RAG, HITL (``cdl_llm.graphs``)
17nm|- **Observability** — Tracing, cost tracking, structured logging (``cdl_llm.observability``)
18qp|- **Evaluation** — Metrics, golden datasets, eval runner (``cdl_llm.eval``)
19th|
20ft|Quick start::
21st|
22md|    from cdl_llm.config import get_config
23zb|    from cdl_llm.llm.factory import get_llm
24th|
25hi|    cfg = get_config()
26ze|    llm = get_llm(cfg)
27ws|    # llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello!")])
28th|
29gs|Version: ``0.1.0``
30vn|"""
31st|

1wt|"""Base type definitions shared across cdl_llm.
2nd|
3gb|Contains fundamental enumerations and the core LLM response model.
4th|
5dl|Example::
6th|
7lg|    from cdl_llm.types import MessageRole, BackendProvider, LLMResponse
8oe|"""
9th|
10kt|from __future__ import annotations
11th|
12ur|from enum import Enum
13bm|from typing import Any
14th|
15im|from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
16th|
17th|
18uk|# -- Enumerations -------------------------------------------------------------
19th|
20th|
21hd|class MessageRole(str, Enum):
22lj|    """Standard roles used in LLM chat messages."""
23rd|
24vh|    SYSTEM = "system"
25lr|    USER = "user"
26wy|    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
27cf|    TOOL = "tool"
28th|
29th|
30lo|class BackendProvider(str, Enum):
31fi|    """Canonical provider names used in factory registries."""
32nd|
33gs|    OPENAI = "openai"
34of|    ANTHROPIC = "anthropic"
35cl|    OLLAMA = "ollama"
36qr|    LOCAL = "local"
37aa|    QDRANT = "qdrant"
38oe|    CHROMA = "chroma"
39dd|    PGVECTOR = "pgvector"
40hq|    REDIS = "redis"
41qy|    POSTGRES = "postgres"
42cs|    IN_MEMORY = "in_memory"
43rd|
44th|
45ss|# -- LLM types ----------------------------------------------------------------
46th|
47th|
48dw|class LLMResponse(BaseModel):
49ln|    """Structured response from an LLM backend call.
50th|
51ov|    Attributes:
52gj|        content: The text content of the response.
53hb|        model: The model name that produced the response.
54jg|        usage_tokens: Token usage breakdown (prompt, completion, total).
55yy|        latency_ms: Wall-clock time of the LLM call in milliseconds.
56ge|        finish_reason: The stop reason reported by the provider.
57ip|        raw: Provider-specific raw response object (not serialized).
58yj|    """
59th|
60wx|    content: str
61ja|    model: str
62wy|    usage_tokens: dict[str, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
63sl|    latency_ms: float = 0.0
64dk|    finish_reason: str | None = None
65zf|    raw: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)
66th|
67ou|    model_config = {"arbitrary_types_allowed": True}
68th|
69th|
70ug|__all__ = [
71gn|    "MessageRole",
72qq|    "BackendProvider",
73pr|    "LLMResponse",
74gr|]

1ah|"""
2ac|Factory and registry for LLM backends.
3rd|
4bt|Register custom backends at runtime without modifying library code::
5th|
6th|    from cdl_llm.llm.factory import register_llm_backend
7bk|    from my_cdl.llm import AzureOpenAIBackend
8th|
9ab|    register_llm_backend("azure", AzureOpenAIBackend)
10th|
11qo|Then use via config::
12th|
13pe|    CDL_LLM_LLM_BACKEND=azure
14th|
15dl|Example::
16th|
17zb|    from cdl_llm.llm.factory import get_llm
18md|    from cdl_llm.config import get_config
19th|
20qd|    llm = get_llm(get_config())
21zd|    response = llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello!")])
22zn|"""
23rd|
24kt|from __future__ import annotations
25th|
26cq|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
27th|
28hk|from ..config import LLMLibConfig, get_config
29lj|from ..exceptions import ConfigurationError
30cd|from .base import BaseLLMBackend
31st|
32zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
33sk|    pass
34th|
35th|
36jh|def _make_openai(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> BaseLLMBackend:
37jg|    from .providers.openai_provider import OpenAIBackend
38th|
39sd|    api_key = cfg.openai_api_key.get_secret_value() if cfg.openai_api_key else None
40zb|    return OpenAIBackend(
41ma|        model=cfg.llm_model,
42ll|        api_key=api_key,
43gd|        temperature=cfg.llm_temperature,
44ox|        max_retries=cfg.llm_max_retries,
45py|        base_url=cfg.llm_base_url,
46ne|    )
47th|
48th|
49uy|def _make_anthropic(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> BaseLLMBackend:
50lz|    from .providers.anthropic_provider import AnthropicBackend
51st|
52ab|    api_key = cfg.anthropic_api_key.get_secret_value() if cfg.anthropic_api_key else None
53vg|    return AnthropicBackend(
54ma|        model=cfg.llm_model,
55ll|        api_key=api_key,
56gd|        temperature=cfg.llm_temperature,
57ox|        max_retries=cfg.llm_max_retries,
58gh|    )
59th|
60th|
61aa|def _make_ollama(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> BaseLLMBackend:
62pl|    from .providers.ollama_provider import OllamaBackend
63rd|
64jr|    return OllamaBackend(
65ma|        model=cfg.llm_model,
66dh|        base_url=cfg.llm_base_url or "http://localhost:11434",
67gd|        temperature=cfg.llm_temperature,
68qb|    )
69th|
70th|
71ls|def _make_google(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> BaseLLMBackend:
72zh|    from .providers.google_provider import GoogleBackend
73rd|
74yx|    api_key = cfg.google_api_key.get_secret_value() if getattr(cfg, "google_api_key", None) else None
75so|    return GoogleBackend(
76ma|        model=cfg.llm_model,
77ll|        api_key=api_key,
78gd|        temperature=cfg.llm_temperature,
79ox|        max_retries=cfg.llm_max_retries,
80lc|    )
81st|
82nd|
83cn|def _make_mistral(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> BaseLLMBackend:
84wu|    from .providers.mistral_provider import MistralBackend
85th|
86fl|    api_key = cfg.mistral_api_key.get_secret_value() if getattr(cfg, "mistral_api_key", None) else None
87ru|    return MistralBackend(
88ma|        model=cfg.llm_model,
89ll|        api_key=api_key,
90gd|        temperature=cfg.llm_temperature,
91ox|        max_retries=cfg.llm_max_retries,
92pb|    )
93rd|
94th|
95hm|#: Registry mapping backend name -> factory callable.
96ux|#: Extend this registry using :func:`register_llm_backend`.
97px|REGISTRY: dict[str, type[BaseLLMBackend] | callable] = {  # type: ignore[type-arg]
98au|    "openai": _make_openai,
99by|    "anthropic": _make_anthropic,
100ai|    "ollama": _make_ollama,
101ht|    "google": _make_google,
102ax|    "mistral": _make_mistral,
103rd|}
104th|
105th|
106nk|def get_llm(config: LLMLibConfig | None = None) -> BaseLLMBackend:
107yo|    """Instantiate and return the configured LLM backend.
108th|
109zn|    Args:
110mq|        config: Library configuration. If ``None``, uses :func:`get_config`.
111th|
112wj|    Returns:
113mz|        A ready-to-use :class:`BaseLLMBackend` instance.
114th|
115ji|    Raises:
116gp|        ConfigurationError: If the requested backend is not in the registry.
117th|
118ow|    Example:
119no|        >>> from cdl_llm.llm.factory import get_llm
120om|        >>> llm = get_llm()  # uses CDL_LLM_LLM_BACKEND env var
121ah|    """
122pa|    cfg = config or get_config()
123fa|    backend_name = cfg.llm_backend.lower()
124lh|    factory = REGISTRY.get(backend_name)
125rv|    if factory is None:
126qc|        available = ", ".join(sorted(str(k) for k in REGISTRY))
127ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
128mu|            f"Unknown LLM backend '{backend_name}'. Available: {available}. "
129qx|            "Register a custom backend with register_llm_backend()."
130vc|        )
131uz|    return factory(cfg)  # type: ignore[operator]
132nd|
133rd|
134jw|def register_llm_backend(name: str, cls: type[BaseLLMBackend]) -> None:
135as|    """Register a custom LLM backend class in the global registry.
136th|
137kp|    This allows third-party or company-specific backends to be used via config
138vt|    without forking the library.
139th|
140zn|    Args:
141jr|        name: The backend name (used as ``CDL_LLM_LLM_BACKEND`` value).
142hs|        cls: A concrete subclass of :class:`BaseLLMBackend`.
143rd|
144ji|    Raises:
145kp|        TypeError: If ``cls`` is not a subclass of :class:`BaseLLMBackend`.
146th|
147ow|    Example:
148ny|        >>> register_llm_backend("my_provider", MyCustomBackend)
149mn|    """
150rn|    if not (isinstance(cls, type) and issubclass(cls, BaseLLMBackend)):
151oh|        raise TypeError(f"{cls!r} must be a subclass of BaseLLMBackend")
152gm|    REGISTRY[name] = cls
153rd|

10th|

22fb|    RateLimitError,
23wb|    RetryExhaustedError,
24fy|    ToolExecutionError,
25pe|    VectorStoreError,
26bx|)
27th|
28ug|__all__ = [
29ql|    "CdlLLMError",
30dp|    "ConfigurationError",
31ll|    "BackendError",
32mp|    "RetryExhaustedError",
33sl|    "RateLimitError",
34uj|    "OutputParsingError",
35mq|    "PromptNotFoundError",
36dc|    "ToolExecutionError",
37fi|    "VectorStoreError",
38pa|    "EvaluationError",
39ce|    "MCPServerError",
40jl|    "MCPClientError",
41fa|    "GraphValidationError",
42ds|    "GraphNodeError",
43hf|]

1ah|"""
2uc|Unified configuration singleton for the cdl_llm library.
3rd|
4uq|All values are read from environment variables with the prefix ``CDL_LLM_``
5pa|(case-insensitive) or from a ``.env`` file in the working directory.
6th|
7ja|Singleton access::
8th|
9md|    from cdl_llm.config import get_config
10th|
11hi|    cfg = get_config()
12kp|    print(cfg.llm_backend)  # "openai"

15th|
16wl|    CDL_LLM_LLM_BACKEND=anthropic
17ny|    CDL_LLM_LLM_MODEL=claude-opus-4-6
18xp|    CDL_LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
19tr|    CDL_LLM_VECTORSTORE_URL=http://localhost:6333

21st|
22kt|from __future__ import annotations
23rd|
24ur|import functools
25th|
26oz|from pydantic import SecretStr
27eo|from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
28th|
29th|
30jp|class LLMLibConfig(BaseSettings):
31jy|    """Unified configuration for every cdl_llm backend.
32nd|
33bl|    Attributes are grouped by the sub-system they control. Override any value
34iz|    via the corresponding ``CDL_LLM_*`` environment variable.

38ar|        >>> cfg = LLMLibConfig()
39bh|        >>> cfg.llm_backend
40eh|        'openai'
41wi|    """
42nd|
43sx|    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
44pr|        env_prefix="CDL_LLM_",
45uv|        env_file=".env",
46re|        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
47dy|        case_sensitive=False,
48oh|        extra="ignore",
49br|    )
50th|

52to|    llm_backend: str = "openai"
53mr|    """LLM backend name: ``openai`` | ``anthropic`` | ``ollama``."""
54th|
55pr|    llm_model: str = "gpt-4o-mini"
56xd|    """Model identifier used by the LLM backend."""
57th|
58ss|    llm_temperature: float = 0.0
59rs|    """Default sampling temperature. 0.0 for deterministic output."""
60th|
61os|    llm_max_retries: int = 3
62yd|    """Maximum retry attempts for failed LLM calls."""
63rd|
64zz|    llm_base_url: str | None = None
65dc|    """Override base URL for the LLM provider (e.g. for Ollama or proxies)."""
66th|
67zn|    # -- Embeddings ------------------------------------------------------------

70th|
71tu|    embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-3-small"
72zg|    """Model identifier used by the embedding backend."""
73rd|
74dh|    embedding_dimensions: int | None = None
75om|    """Override output vector dimensions if the backend supports it."""
76th|
77qt|    # -- Vector store ----------------------------------------------------------
78jy|    vectorstore_backend: str = "qdrant"
79js|    """Vector store backend: ``qdrant`` | ``chroma`` | ``pgvector``."""
80th|
81na|    vectorstore_url: str = "http://localhost:6333"
82ib|    """Connection URL for the vector database."""
83rd|
84ze|    vectorstore_api_key: SecretStr | None = None
85mi|    """API key for the vector database (Qdrant Cloud, etc.)."""
86th|
87xp|    vectorstore_collection: str = "default"
88cs|    """Default collection name."""
89th|
90qc|    collection_prefix: str = ""
91fq|    """Optional prefix applied to all collection names."""
92nd|
93ri|    # -- Chunking --------------------------------------------------------------
94fy|    chunking_strategy: str = "recursive"
95kn|    """Default chunking strategy: ``fixed`` | ``recursive`` | ``semantic``
96qt|    | ``markdown`` | ``code`` | ``none``."""
97th|
98wy|    chunk_size: int = 512
99lg|    """Target chunk size in characters."""

101zr|    chunk_overlap: int = 64
102sb|    """Overlap in characters between consecutive chunks."""
103rd|
104vt|    # -- Memory ----------------------------------------------------------------
105mh|    memory_backend: str = "in_memory"
106mu|    """Conversation memory backend: ``in_memory`` | ``redis`` | ``postgres``."""
107th|
108ri|    memory_redis_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379"
109qi|    """Redis connection URL (used when memory_backend=redis)."""
110th|
111lg|    memory_postgres_url: str | None = None
112zl|    """Postgres DSN (used when memory_backend=postgres)."""
113th|
114gf|    memory_ttl_seconds: int = 86400

116th|
117uu|    # -- Tracing / observability -----------------------------------------------

120th|
121gj|    langsmith_api_key: SecretStr | None = None
122rf|    """LangSmith API key for tracing."""
123rd|
124ik|    langsmith_project: str = "default"
125ct|    """LangSmith project name."""

127je|    otel_endpoint: str = "http://localhost:4317"

130ri|    # -- Credentials -----------------------------------------------------------

133rd|
134ry|    anthropic_api_key: SecretStr | None = None

137ve|    # -- Semantic cache --------------------------------------------------------

160re|    """

162as|    mcp_transport: str = "streamable_http"
163tt|    """Transport protocol for MCP connections. ``streamable_http`` | ``stdio``."""
164th|
165zw|    # -- Legacy aliases (backwards-compat with existing env vars) ---------------

1gu|# DEPENDENCY POLICY — read before adding any dependency
2pj|#
3hu|# This library follows a three-tier dependency model:
4gn|#
5cy|# TIER 1 — LangChain infrastructure packages (use confidently)
6ue|#   langchain-core, langgraph, langchain-text-splitters, and all
7vr|#   langchain-{provider} packages (langchain-openai, langchain-anthropic, etc.)
8bu|#   are co-maintained by LangChain AND the providers themselves. They have
9vs|#   independently versioned releases and absorb raw SDK API changes for us.
10vx|#   Our job is governance and abstraction — NOT maintaining raw SDK wrappers.
11kd|#
12dl|# TIER 2 — Specialized libraries used directly (not via LangChain)
13vp|#   instructor, opentelemetry-sdk, ragas, deepeval, structlog.
14im|#   These are purpose-built for their domain. LangChain adds no value over
15dc|#   them, so we depend on them directly.
16tn|#
17lx|# TIER 3 — NEVER USE (hard rule, PR will be rejected)
18ss|#   - "langchain"              meta-package, pins everything, avoid
19tk|#   - "langchain-community"    inconsistent quality, community-maintained
20ij|#   - LLMChain, RetrievalQA,   opaque chain classes that hide control flow;
21gv|#     ConversationalRetrievalChain  use LangGraph instead
22fh|#   - Any raw provider SDK     openai, anthropic, etc. go through their
23he|#     (openai, anthropic, ...)  langchain-{provider} wrapper only
24sg|#
25ss|# UPGRADE CONTRACT: when langchain-{provider} releases a breaking change,
26vp|# only the concrete backend file (e.g. llm/openai.py) needs updating —
27oa|# no changes to ABCs, no changes to callers.
28th|
29aj|[build-system]
30hg|requires = ["hatchling"]
31gp|build-backend = "hatchling.build"
32nd|
33cp|[project]
34oe|name = "cdl-llm"
35dn|version = "0.1.0"
36cq|description = "Internal LLM platform library — abstraction layer for all LLM components"
37nn|readme = "README.md"
38gw|requires-python = ">=3.11"
39qi|license = { text = "Proprietary" }
40sb|authors = [{ name = "Your Company AI Platform Team" }]
41st|
42mt|dependencies = [
43ux|  # Tier 1 — LangChain infrastructure (maintained by LangChain + providers)
44fz|  "langchain-core>=0.3",
45to|  "langchain-text-splitters>=0.3",
46dc|  "langgraph>=0.2",
47th|
48zh|  # Tier 2 — Foundational utilities (always installed)
49fx|  "pydantic>=2.6",
50fl|  "pydantic-settings>=2.2",
51ti|  "httpx>=0.27",
52pl|  "tenacity>=8.3",
53pw|  "structlog>=24.1",
54hx|  "python-dotenv>=1.0",
55ma|  "jinja2>=3.1",
56pk|]
57th|
58ds|[project.optional-dependencies]
59tu|# ── Tier 1: Provider integrations via langchain packages ─────────────────────
60hd|# Each langchain-{provider} package is independently versioned and tracks the
61cj|# provider's API changes. We do NOT directly depend on openai, anthropic, etc.
62kr|openai      = ["langchain-openai>=0.2"]
63kw|anthropic   = ["langchain-anthropic>=0.2"]
64ae|ollama      = ["langchain-ollama>=0.2"]
65zi|qdrant      = ["langchain-qdrant>=0.1", "qdrant-client>=1.9"]
66nn|chroma      = ["langchain-chroma>=0.1", "chromadb>=0.5"]
67zd|pgvector    = ["langchain-postgres>=0.0.9", "psycopg[binary]>=3.1"]
68hq|redis       = ["langchain-redis>=0.1", "redis>=5.0"]
69tj|# NOTE: local uses sentence-transformers directly — no langchain-community
70uu|local       = ["sentence-transformers>=3.0"]
71st|
72ru|# ── Tier 2: Specialized tools used directly (not via LangChain) ──────────────
73sw|# instructor: purpose-built for structured LLM output with Pydantic v2 validation
74me|# and automatic retry — superior to LangChain's output parsers for production use
75vy|structured  = ["instructor>=1.0"]
76mx|# pydantic-ai: schema-first agent framework for structured, type-safe agentic tasks
77xb|pydantic-ai = ["pydantic-ai>=0.0.14"]
78mp|# opentelemetry-sdk: industry-standard observability, provider-agnostic
79xd|otel        = ["opentelemetry-sdk>=1.24", "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp>=1.24"]
80hs|# langsmith: LangChain's hosted tracing platform
81lk|langsmith   = ["langsmith>=0.1"]
82qd|# ragas + deepeval: purpose-built RAG and LLM evaluation frameworks
83to|eval        = ["ragas>=0.1", "deepeval>=0.21"]
84mv|# MCP: fastmcp for server-side tool exposure, langchain-mcp-adapters for agent consumption
85ck|mcp         = ["fastmcp>=2.0", "langchain-mcp-adapters>=0.1"]
86th|
87tr|# ── Development tooling ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
88tc|dev = [
89mf|  "pytest>=8.0",
90la|  "pytest-asyncio>=0.23",
91lg|  "pytest-cov>=5.0",
92kh|  "ruff>=0.4",
93ch|  "mypy>=1.10",
94hy|  "cdl-llm[openai,qdrant,local,eval,structured,pydantic-ai,mcp]",
95rg|]
96qp|all = [
97bb|  "cdl-llm[openai,anthropic,ollama,qdrant,chroma,pgvector,local,redis,otel,langsmith,eval,structured,pydantic-ai,mcp]",
98yy|]
99th|
100we|[project.urls]
101fx|Repository = "https://github.com/your-org/co-creation-framework"
102nd|
103hq|[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
104se|packages = ["cdl_llm"]
105th|
106mr|[tool.pytest.ini_options]
107yc|asyncio_mode = "auto"
108ij|testpaths = ["tests"]
109qd|addopts = "--cov=cdl_llm --cov-report=term-missing"
110th|
111rf|[tool.ruff]
112ln|line-length = 100
113qi|target-version = "py311"
114th|
115qb|[tool.ruff.lint]
116fd|select = ["E", "F", "I", "ANN", "UP", "B"]
117rd|ignore = ["ANN101", "ANN102"]
118th|

121eh|strict = true

1ah|"""
2um|Abstract base class for LLM backends.
3rd|
4zo|All LLM implementations must inherit from :class:`BaseLLMBackend` and implement
5ye|the :attr:`_chat_model` property, which returns the underlying
6ss|:class:`langchain_core.language_models.BaseChatModel` instance.
7th|
8zf|**Why this design?**
9th|
10uj|All public methods (``invoke``, ``ainvoke``, ``stream``, ``astream``,
11hv|``with_structured_output``) are concrete and delegate to ``self._chat_model``.
12no|This means:
13th|
14el|* Subclasses only override ``_chat_model`` (plus ``model_name`` / ``provider``).
15ny|* When ``langchain-openai`` or ``langchain-anthropic`` releases a breaking change,
16nf|  only the concrete backend file (``openai.py``, ``anthropic.py``) needs updating —
17ze|  the ABC and all callers remain untouched.
18vq|* The ``BaseChatModel`` interface is LangGraph-native; backends work in any
19yv|  LangGraph graph without any adaptation.
20th|
21dl|Example::
22nd|
23mv|    from cdl_llm.llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
24pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
25th|
26ew|    llm = OpenAIBackend(model="gpt-4o-mini")
27an|    response = llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
28iq|"""
29th|
30kt|from __future__ import annotations
31st|
32ci|import abc
33dt|from collections.abc import AsyncIterator, Iterator
34wb|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
35th|
36tu|from langchain_core.language_models import BaseChatModel
37xs|from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, AIMessageChunk, BaseMessage
38th|
39zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
40xp|    from pydantic import BaseModel
41st|
42nd|
43gd|class BaseLLMBackend(abc.ABC):
44sv|    """Abstract interface for all LLM provider backends.
45th|
46pa|    Concrete subclasses must implement:
47th|
48hy|    * :attr:`_chat_model` — returns the underlying :class:`~langchain_core.language_models.BaseChatModel`
49tl|    * :attr:`model_name`  — returns the model identifier string
50rk|    * :attr:`provider`    — returns the provider name (``"openai"``, ``"anthropic"``, …)
51st|
52if|    All request methods (``invoke``, ``ainvoke``, ``stream``, ``astream``,
53jj|    ``with_structured_output``) are concrete and call through to the underlying
54sw|    ``BaseChatModel``.  **Do not re-implement them in subclasses.**
55th|
56ow|    Example:
57af|        >>> llm = OpenAIBackend(model="gpt-4o-mini")
58nl|        >>> msg = llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content="2+2?")])
59xr|        >>> msg.content
60ug|        '4'
61rt|    """
62nd|
63cs|    # ── Required overrides ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64th|
65jq|    @property
66bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
67ah|    def _chat_model(self) -> BaseChatModel:
68cg|        """The underlying langchain-core chat model instance.
69th|
70pj|        All public request methods delegate to this object.  Subclasses must
71ab|        store the concrete provider model here (e.g. ``ChatOpenAI``,
72fn|        ``ChatAnthropic``, ``ChatOllama``).
73rd|
74rt|        Returns:
75zn|            A :class:`~langchain_core.language_models.BaseChatModel` instance.
76pj|        """
77th|
78jq|    @property
79bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
80tn|    def model_name(self) -> str:
81fx|        """The model identifier string (e.g. ``"gpt-4o-mini"``)."""
82nd|
83jq|    @property
84bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
85fb|    def provider(self) -> str:
86rl|        """The provider name (e.g. ``"openai"``, ``"anthropic"``, ``"ollama"``)."""
87th|
88rc|    # ── Concrete delegation methods — do NOT override in subclasses ───────────
89th|
90bk|    def invoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
91ci|        """Invoke the LLM synchronously.
92nd|
93ld|        Delegates to ``self._chat_model.invoke()``.
94th|
95oz|        Args:
96tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
97zc|            **kwargs: Backend-specific overrides (temperature, max_tokens, etc.).
98th|
99rt|        Returns:
100jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
101st|
102nu|        Raises:
103bg|            BackendError: If the provider returns an error.
104fy|        """
105gu|        return self._chat_model.invoke(messages, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[return-value]
106th|
107py|    async def ainvoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
108hc|        """Invoke the LLM asynchronously.
109th|
110ig|        Delegates to ``self._chat_model.ainvoke()``.
111th|
112oz|        Args:
113tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
114pa|            **kwargs: Backend-specific overrides.
115th|
116rt|        Returns:
117jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
118th|
119nu|        Raises:
120bg|            BackendError: If the provider returns an error.
121te|        """
122eb|        return await self._chat_model.ainvoke(messages, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[return-value]
123rd|
124rk|    def stream(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[AIMessageChunk]:
125fd|        """Stream response chunks synchronously.
126th|
127lx|        Delegates to ``self._chat_model.stream()``.
128th|
129oz|        Args:
130tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
131pa|            **kwargs: Backend-specific overrides.
132nd|
133qm|        Yields:
134dz|            :class:`AIMessageChunk` objects as they arrive from the provider.
135th|
136nu|        Raises:
137fv|            BackendError: If the stream fails.
138bj|        """
139wj|        yield from self._chat_model.stream(messages, **kwargs)
140th|
141de|    async def astream(
142dp|        self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any
143uw|    ) -> AsyncIterator[AIMessageChunk]:
144uz|        """Stream response chunks asynchronously.
145th|
146or|        Delegates to ``self._chat_model.astream()``.
147th|
148oz|        Args:
149tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
150pa|            **kwargs: Backend-specific overrides.
151st|
152qm|        Yields:
153dz|            :class:`AIMessageChunk` objects as they arrive from the provider.
154th|
155nu|        Raises:
156fv|            BackendError: If the stream fails.
157nm|        """
158ly|        async for chunk in self._chat_model.astream(messages, **kwargs):
159ah|            yield chunk  # type: ignore[misc]
160th|
161xx|    def with_structured_output(self, schema: type[BaseModel]) -> Any:
162lu|        """Return a LangChain Runnable configured to produce structured output.
163rd|
164un|        Delegates to ``self._chat_model.with_structured_output()``, which uses
165bk|        the provider's native JSON schema / function-calling mode.
166th|
167ks|        For production-grade structured output with Pydantic v2 validation and
168ya|        automatic retry, prefer :func:`cdl_llm.output.structured.get_structured_output`
169ig|        which uses ``instructor`` under the hood.
170th|
171oz|        Args:
172fy|            schema: A Pydantic ``BaseModel`` subclass describing the output.
173rd|
174rt|        Returns:
175km|            A LangChain ``Runnable`` that produces instances of ``schema``.
176dc|        """
177op|        return self._chat_model.with_structured_output(schema)
178th|
179th|
180le|# ── Legacy dataclasses — kept for backward compatibility ──────────────────────
181st|
182il|from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field  # noqa: E402
183rd|
184th|
185pj|@dataclass
186ig|class LLMMessage:
187mt|    """Legacy message dataclass. Prefer :class:`langchain_core.messages.BaseMessage`."""
188th|
189ul|    role: str  # "system" | "user" | "assistant"
190wx|    content: str
191st|
192nd|
193pj|@dataclass
194dj|class LLMResponse:
195tv|    """Legacy response dataclass. Prefer :class:`cdl_llm.types.LLMResponse`."""
196th|
197wx|    content: str
198ja|    model: str
199cx|    usage: dict[str, int] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)
200lm|    raw: Any = None
201st|

7th|

12rg|"""

15th|
16hg|from .base_agent import AgentInput, AgentOutput, BaseAgent

1ah|"""
2fw|Abstract base class and shared data models for all agents.
3rd|
4oh|Every agent implementation must inherit from :class:`BaseAgent` and implement
5db|``invoke``, ``ainvoke``, and ``stream``.
6th|
7dl|Example::
8th|
9vj|    from cdl_llm.agents.base_agent import BaseAgent, AgentInput, AgentOutput
10th|
11es|    class MyAgent(BaseAgent):
12ek|        def invoke(self, input: AgentInput) -> AgentOutput:
13mk|            return AgentOutput(result="Hello!", success=True)
14nx|"""
15th|
16kt|from __future__ import annotations
17th|
18ci|import abc
19rf|from collections.abc import Iterator
20bm|from typing import Any
21st|
22im|from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
23rd|
24th|
25bi|class AgentInput(BaseModel):
26vh|    """Input payload for any agent invocation.
27th|
28ov|    Attributes:
29ww|        task: The primary task or question for the agent.
30du|        context: Optional background context to inform the response.
31wi|        session_id: Session identifier for memory retrieval.
32su|        metadata: Arbitrary metadata forwarded to the agent.
33zs|    """
34th|
35ol|    task: str
36pd|    context: str | None = None
37xn|    session_id: str | None = None
38jb|    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
39th|
40th|
41sc|class AgentOutput(BaseModel):
42my|    """Output payload from any agent invocation.
43rd|
44ov|    Attributes:
45ow|        result: The final answer or output from the agent.
46hg|        reasoning_steps: Ordered list of intermediate reasoning steps or thoughts.
47zx|        tools_used: Names of tools invoked during execution.
48tv|        token_usage: Token usage breakdown (prompt, completion, total).
49aa|        success: ``True`` if the agent completed without fatal errors.
50tr|        error: Error message if ``success`` is ``False``.
51ns|    """
52nd|
53kl|    result: str
54yh|    reasoning_steps: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
55yg|    tools_used: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
56hz|    token_usage: dict[str, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
57vl|    success: bool = True
58fx|    error: str | None = None
59th|
60th|
61oc|class BaseAgent(abc.ABC):
62zo|    """Abstract interface for all agent implementations.
63rd|
64rs|    All agents receive an :class:`AgentInput` and return an :class:`AgentOutput`.
65af|    The streaming variant yields partial outputs as execution progresses.
66fp|    """
67th|
68bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
69zg|    def invoke(self, input: AgentInput) -> AgentOutput:
70pg|        """Run the agent synchronously and return the final output.
71st|
72oz|        Args:
73tj|            input: The task and context for the agent.
74th|
75rt|        Returns:
76jc|            :class:`AgentOutput` with the result and execution metadata.
77th|
78nu|        Raises:
79od|            BackendError: If the underlying LLM call fails unrecoverably.
80oa|        """
81st|
82bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
83fg|    async def ainvoke(self, input: AgentInput) -> AgentOutput:
84ye|        """Run the agent asynchronously and return the final output.
85th|
86oz|        Args:
87tj|            input: The task and context for the agent.
88th|
89rt|        Returns:
90jc|            :class:`AgentOutput` with the result and execution metadata.
91st|
92nu|        Raises:
93od|            BackendError: If the underlying LLM call fails unrecoverably.
94hx|        """
95th|
96bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
97si|    def stream(self, input: AgentInput) -> Iterator[AgentOutput]:
98vn|        """Stream partial agent outputs as they are produced.
99th|
100oz|        Args:
101tj|            input: The task and context for the agent.
102nd|
103qm|        Yields:
104zc|            :class:`AgentOutput` objects for each significant step.
105jg|            The final yielded output has ``success=True`` (or ``False`` on error).
106mu|        """
107th|

5th|
6iq|    from cdl_llm.memory.base import BaseMemoryBackend
7th|
8zz|continues to work without modification.
9jl|"""
10th|

12th|

1ah|"""
2ka|Type definitions specific to the LLM module.
3rd|
4av|These types extend the shared :mod:`cdl_llm.types` with LLM-specific
5rz|structures such as tool call schemas and streaming chunk types.
6jc|"""
7th|
8kt|from __future__ import annotations
9th|
10bm|from typing import Any
11th|
12im|from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
13th|
14th|
15nb|class ToolCall(BaseModel):
16xa|    """A tool call requested by the LLM.
17th|
18ov|    Attributes:
19sy|        id: Provider-assigned tool call identifier.
20th|        name: The name of the tool to invoke.
21nm|        arguments: JSON-parsed arguments to pass to the tool.
22uc|    """
23rd|
24rn|    id: str
25hl|    name: str
26im|    arguments: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
27th|
28th|
29rv|class StreamChunk(BaseModel):
30rg|    """A single chunk from a streaming LLM response.
31st|
32ov|    Attributes:
33yx|        content: Partial text content in this chunk.
34jj|        tool_calls: Any tool call deltas included in this chunk.
35uv|        finish_reason: Set on the final chunk when the stream ends.
36zm|        model: The model producing the stream (may be empty on mid-stream chunks).
37ge|    """
38th|
39sb|    content: str = ""
40fh|    tool_calls: list[ToolCall] = Field(default_factory=list)
41dk|    finish_reason: str | None = None
42ww|    model: str = ""
43rd|
44th|
45sq|class UsageMetadata(BaseModel):
46vx|    """Token usage metadata from an LLM call.
47th|
48ov|    Attributes:
49wm|        prompt_tokens: Tokens consumed by the input prompt.
50yp|        completion_tokens: Tokens generated in the response.
51ge|        total_tokens: Sum of prompt and completion tokens.
52cw|    """
53rd|
54uj|    prompt_tokens: int = 0
55iv|    completion_tokens: int = 0
56si|    total_tokens: int = 0
57th|
58uy|    @classmethod
59ny|    def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, int]) -> UsageMetadata:
60ss|        """Construct from a raw provider usage dict.
61st|
62oz|        Args:
63yn|            data: Dict with keys like ``prompt_tokens``, ``completion_tokens``.
64th|
65rt|        Returns:
66vm|            Populated :class:`UsageMetadata` instance.
67uw|        """
68gf|        return cls(
69cr|            prompt_tokens=data.get("prompt_tokens", 0),
70sr|            completion_tokens=data.get("completion_tokens", 0),
71jp|            total_tokens=data.get("total_tokens", data.get("prompt_tokens", 0) + data.get("completion_tokens", 0)),
72si|        )
73rd|
74th|
75do|__all__ = ["ToolCall", "StreamChunk", "UsageMetadata"]
76th|

1ah|"""
2ze|Abstract base class for all cdl_llm tools.
3rd|
4il|Tools are the callable units that agents can invoke during reasoning.
5md|Each tool wraps a concrete capability (web search, SQL query, file I/O, etc.)
6wr|and exposes it through a uniform interface compatible with LangChain's tool
7gw|abstraction.
8th|
9dl|Example::
10th|
11hx|    from cdl_llm.tools.base import BaseTool
12th|
13qb|    class EchoTool(BaseTool):
14rc|        @property
15iw|        def name(self) -> str:
16ng|            return "echo"
17th|
18rc|        @property
19re|        def description(self) -> str:
20fc|            return "Echo the input string back to the caller."
21st|
22ez|        def run(self, input: str) -> str:
23xt|            return input
24th|
25ku|    tool = EchoTool()
26sw|    lc_tool = tool.to_langchain()
27kv|"""
28th|
29kt|from __future__ import annotations
30th|
31ci|import abc
32at|import asyncio
33wb|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
34th|
35zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
36xp|    from pydantic import BaseModel
37th|
38th|
39px|class BaseTool(abc.ABC):
40ss|    """Abstract interface for all tool implementations.
41st|
42cv|    Every tool must provide a snake_case ``name``, a verbose ``description``
43pb|    (used verbatim in agent system prompts), and a synchronous ``run`` method.
44hr|    The async ``arun`` is provided as a default that delegates to ``run`` via
45rt|    an executor so subclasses only need to override it for truly async I/O.
46th|
47nh|    The ``to_langchain`` method converts the tool to a LangChain-compatible
48df|    object that can be bound to any LangChain / LangGraph agent.
49th|
50ov|    Attributes:
51ts|        name: Snake_case identifier for the tool.
52to|        description: Verbose description used in agent prompts.
53fo|        args_schema: Optional Pydantic model describing the tool's input.
54kj|    """
55th|
56jq|    @property
57bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
58xe|    def name(self) -> str:
59xe|        """Snake_case identifier used to reference this tool.
60th|
61rt|        Returns:
62jb|            A non-empty snake_case string, e.g. ``"web_search"``.
63ya|        """
64th|
65jq|    @property
66bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
67az|    def description(self) -> str:
68zx|        """Verbose description used verbatim in agent system prompts.
69th|
70dl|        The description should explain:
71mo|        - What the tool does.
72lm|        - When to use it.
73rk|        - What the input format is.
74ki|        - What the output looks like.
75th|
76rt|        Returns:
77pg|            A non-empty string suitable for inclusion in an agent prompt.
78uz|        """
79th|
80jq|    @property
81im|    def args_schema(self) -> type[BaseModel] | None:
82qn|        """Optional Pydantic model that describes the tool's input schema.
83rd|
84hx|        When provided, LangChain uses this to generate a JSON schema for
85kf|        function/tool calling APIs such as OpenAI's tool-calling interface.
86th|
87rt|        Returns:
88cn|            A Pydantic :class:`~pydantic.BaseModel` subclass, or ``None`` if
89re|            the tool accepts a plain string input.
90fe|        """
91ki|        return None
92nd|
93bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
94hy|    def run(self, input: str) -> str:
95gz|        """Execute the tool synchronously.
96th|
97oz|        Args:
98hf|            input: The raw string input from the agent. For structured tools,
99ba|                this is typically a JSON string; for simple tools, a plain
100rt|                query string.
101st|
102rt|        Returns:
103fa|            The tool's result as a string, to be included in the agent's
104ga|            message history.
105th|
106nu|        Raises:
107vo|            ToolExecutionError: If the tool fails to execute successfully.
108yh|        """
109th|
110qc|    async def arun(self, input: str) -> str:
111sa|        """Execute the tool asynchronously.
112th|
113mf|        The default implementation runs :meth:`run` in a thread-pool executor
114wv|        so it does not block the event loop. Override this method for tools
115uq|        that natively support async I/O.
116th|
117oz|        Args:
118gp|            input: The raw string input from the agent.
119th|
120rt|        Returns:
121qb|            The tool's result as a string.
122nd|
123nu|        Raises:
124vo|            ToolExecutionError: If the tool fails to execute successfully.
125th|
126sn|        Example:
127nb|            >>> import asyncio
128fo|            >>> result = asyncio.run(tool.arun("some query"))
129vq|        """
130wx|        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
131ss|        return await loop.run_in_executor(None, self.run, input)
132nd|
133xi|    def to_langchain(self) -> Any:
134ff|        """Convert this tool to a LangChain-compatible tool object.
135th|
136ra|        Builds a :class:`langchain_core.tools.StructuredTool` when
137oe|        ``args_schema`` is set, or a plain :class:`langchain_core.tools.Tool`
138fp|        for string-in / string-out tools.  The resulting object can be passed
139um|        directly to any LangChain agent or ``bind_tools`` call.
140th|
141rt|        Returns:
142fh|            A LangChain tool object compatible with LangChain / LangGraph
143yo|            agents.
144th|
145nu|        Raises:
146th|            ConfigurationError: If ``langchain-core`` is not installed.
147th|
148sn|        Example:
149cf|            >>> lc_tool = my_tool.to_langchain()
150cb|            >>> agent = create_react_agent(llm, tools=[lc_tool])
151pw|        """
152gf|        try:
153vs|            from langchain_core.tools import StructuredTool, Tool
154ei|        except ImportError as exc:
155uj|            from cdl_llm.exceptions import ConfigurationError
156th|
157hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
158of|                "langchain-core is required for to_langchain(). "
159up|                "Install with: pip install langchain-core"
160sj|            ) from exc
161st|
162uy|        schema = self.args_schema
163cz|        if schema is not None:
164mt|            return StructuredTool.from_function(
165jj|                func=self.run,
166ny|                coroutine=self.arun,
167km|                name=self.name,
168qp|                description=self.description,
169ur|                args_schema=schema,
170jo|            )
171st|
172gm|        return Tool(
173ow|            name=self.name,
174kc|            description=self.description,
175hl|            func=self.run,
176pn|            coroutine=self.arun,
177ff|        )
178th|
179th|
180wl|__all__ = ["BaseTool"]
181st|

1ah|"""
2sy|Custom exception hierarchy for the cdl_llm library.
3rd|
4gp|All exceptions inherit from :class:`CdlLLMError` so callers can
5eg|catch the entire library's error surface with a single ``except`` clause
6jq|when appropriate.
7th|
8dl|Example::
9th|
10aq|    from cdl_llm.exceptions import ConfigurationError
11th|
12dy|    raise ConfigurationError("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set")
13bm|"""
14th|
15kt|from __future__ import annotations
16th|
17th|
18uk|class CdlLLMError(Exception):
19bs|    """Base exception for all cdl_llm errors.
20th|
21zn|    Args:
22yy|        detail: Human-readable description of the error.
23ck|    """
24th|
25qy|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "") -> None:
26ap|        self.detail = detail
27mz|        super().__init__(detail)
28th|
29ix|    def __str__(self) -> str:
30da|        return f"[{self.__class__.__name__}] {self.detail}"
31st|
32nd|
33xa|# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34th|
35th|
36py|class ConfigurationError(CdlLLMError):
37mg|    """Raised when required configuration is missing or invalid.
38th|
39jj|    Common causes: missing environment variables, incompatible backend
40fe|    settings, or an unknown backend name passed to a factory.
41st|
42kg|    Example::
43rd|
44ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
45lv|            "CDL_LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY is required for the openai backend"
46ch|        )
47gu|    """
48th|
49th|
50rw|# ── Backend / provider errors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
51st|
52nd|
53tj|class BackendError(CdlLLMError):
54hn|    """Raised when an upstream LLM provider returns an error.
55th|
56sd|    This is the base for all provider-level failures. Catch this class
57kh|    to handle any remote backend issue uniformly.
58yj|    """
59th|
60th|
61gs|class RetryExhaustedError(BackendError):
62mc|    """Raised when all retry attempts for a backend call have failed.
63rd|
64zn|    Args:
65uc|        detail: Summary of the last error encountered.
66np|        attempts: Number of attempts made before giving up.
67th|
68kg|    Example::
69th|
70nb|        raise RetryExhaustedError(
71lu|            "OpenAI returned 429 after 3 attempts", attempts=3
72si|        )
73ct|    """
74th|
75sn|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "", attempts: int = 0) -> None:
76gu|        self.attempts = attempts
77mz|        super().__init__(detail)
78th|
79ix|    def __str__(self) -> str:
80ae|        return f"[{self.__class__.__name__}] After {self.attempts} attempts: {self.detail}"
81st|
82nd|
83pv|class RateLimitError(BackendError):
84ks|    """Raised when a rate limit (token bucket or provider-side) is exhausted.
85th|
86kg|    Example::
87th|
88ez|        raise RateLimitError("Token bucket exhausted: 10000 TPM limit reached")
89es|    """
90th|
91st|
92py|# ── Output / parsing errors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
93rd|
94th|
95ci|class OutputParsingError(CdlLLMError):
96jx|    """Raised when the LLM output cannot be parsed into the expected format.
97th|
98zn|    Args:
99qi|        detail: Description of the parse failure.
100je|        raw_output: The raw LLM output that failed to parse.
101st|
102kg|    Example::
103rd|
104qu|        raise OutputParsingError(
105qm|            "Expected JSON but got plain text",
106zx|            raw_output=llm_text,
107rp|        )
108ao|    """
109th|
110ew|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "", raw_output: str = "") -> None:
111fn|        self.raw_output = raw_output
112mz|        super().__init__(detail)
113th|
114ix|    def __str__(self) -> str:
115tg|        preview = self.raw_output[:120].replace("\n", " ") if self.raw_output else ""
116zb|        suffix = f" | raw: {preview!r}" if preview else ""
117li|        return f"[{self.__class__.__name__}] {self.detail}{suffix}"
118th|
119th|
120cy|# ── Prompt errors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
121st|
122nd|
123qr|class PromptNotFoundError(CdlLLMError):
124mi|    """Raised when a prompt key or version is not found in the registry.
125th|
126zn|    Args:

136dy|        self.key = key
137mv|        self.version = version
138yy|        version_str = f" (version={version})" if version else ""
139jq|        super().__init__(f"Prompt '{key}'{version_str} not found in registry")
140th|
141st|
142nm|# ── Tool errors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143rd|
144th|
145ov|class ToolExecutionError(CdlLLMError):
146rp|    """Raised when a tool raises an exception during execution.
147th|
148zn|    Args:
149cn|        tool_name: The name of the tool that failed.
150jn|        detail: Description of the failure.

152nd|
153kg|    Example::
154th|
155wx|        raise ToolExecutionError("sql_query", "Query returned no results")
156mx|    """
157th|
158fs|    def __init__(
159ss|        self,
160qs|        tool_name: str = "",
161yb|        detail: str = "",
162py|        original: BaseException | None = None,
163px|    ) -> None:
164je|        self.tool_name = tool_name

166jc|        super().__init__(f"Tool '{tool_name}' failed: {detail}")
167th|
168th|
169my|# ── Vector store errors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170th|
171st|
172pn|class VectorStoreError(CdlLLMError):
173op|    """Raised when a vector database operation fails.

175zn|    Args:
176jn|        detail: Description of the failure.

178th|
179kg|    Example::
180th|

182jx|    """
183rd|
184br|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "", backend: str = "") -> None:
185il|        self.backend = backend
186gy|        prefix = f"[{backend}] " if backend else ""
187mw|        super().__init__(f"{prefix}{detail}")
188th|
189th|
190ri|# ── Evaluation errors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191st|
192nd|
193qb|class EvaluationError(CdlLLMError):
194my|    """Raised when the evaluation pipeline encounters an unrecoverable error.
195th|
196kg|    Example::
197th|
198aw|        raise EvaluationError("Dataset is empty — cannot run evaluation")
199va|    """
200th|
201st|
202ig|# ── MCP errors ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
203rd|
204th|
205pg|class MCPServerError(CdlLLMError):

208zn|    Args:

216th|
217ru|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "", server_name: str = "") -> None:
218jq|        self.server_name = server_name

221st|
222nd|
223fk|class MCPClientError(CdlLLMError):
224za|    """Raised when the MCP client fails to connect or load tools from a server.
225th|
226zn|    Args:
227jn|        detail: Description of the failure.
228bg|        server_url: The URL of the MCP server that could not be reached.
229th|
230kg|    Example::

232zw|        raise MCPClientError("Connection refused", server_url="http://rag-service:8001/mcp")

234th|
235br|    def __init__(self, detail: str = "", server_url: str = "") -> None:
236ty|        self.server_url = server_url
237mj|        prefix = f"[{server_url}] " if server_url else ""

239th|
240th|
241im|# ── Graph errors ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
242nd|
243rd|
244xa|class GraphValidationError(CdlLLMError):
245ko|    """Raised when a graph fails structural validation before compilation.
246th|
247pf|    Contains a list of all validation errors found so the developer
248ad|    can fix all issues at once rather than one at a time.

252nd|
253kg|    Example::

256lk|            pipeline.compile()

259px|                print(f"  - {error}")

261st|
262ok|    def __init__(self, errors: list[str]) -> None:
263fu|        self.errors = errors

265ha|        super().__init__(
266az|            f"Graph validation failed with {len(errors)} error(s):\n{formatted}"
267mm|        )
268th|
269th|
270xm|class GraphNodeError(CdlLLMError):

281st|
282pm|    def __init__(self, node_name: str, detail: str) -> None:

285th|

1ah|"""
2ni|Rule-based LLM router for directing calls to different backends.
3rd|
4xm|Evaluates a priority-ordered list of :class:`RoutingRule` objects against a
5wf|:class:`RoutingContext` and dispatches to the appropriate backend.  Rules are
6dl|evaluated in ascending ``priority`` order; the first matching rule wins.
7th|
8dl|Example::
9th|
10wg|    from cdl_llm.gateway.router import LLMRouter, RoutingContext, RoutingRule
11pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
12th|
13cr|    router = LLMRouter(
14us|        rules=[
15fb|            RoutingRule(
16tf|                condition=lambda ctx: ctx.task_type == "code",
17pa|                backend_name="openai",
18ng|                priority=10,
19ed|            ),
20au|        ],
21kg|        backends={"openai": openai_backend, "anthropic": anthropic_backend},
22ic|        default_backend="anthropic",
23ky|    )
24th|
25ed|    context = RoutingContext(task_type="code", estimated_tokens=500)
26fh|    response = router.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Write a sort function")], context)
27kv|"""
28th|
29kt|from __future__ import annotations
30th|
31dt|from collections.abc import AsyncIterator, Iterator
32gw|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable
33rd|
34xs|from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, AIMessageChunk, BaseMessage
35hl|from pydantic import BaseModel
36th|
37dk|from ..exceptions import BackendError, ConfigurationError
38fd|from ..llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
39th|
40zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
41sk|    pass
42nd|
43rd|
44fg|class RoutingContext(BaseModel):
45tu|    """Contextual metadata used to select an LLM backend.
46th|
47ov|    Attributes:
48ow|        task_type: The nature of the task (e.g. ``"qa"``, ``"code"``,
49dc|            ``"summary"``, ``"agent"``).  ``None`` means unspecified.
50we|        estimated_tokens: Rough token count for the request.  Used to route
51na|            large requests to backends with higher context limits.
52al|        environment: Deployment environment.  Can be used to force cheaper
53cb|            backends in non-production environments.
54pm|        latency_budget_ms: Maximum acceptable response latency in milliseconds.
55br|            ``None`` means no constraint.
56th|
57ow|    Example:
58on|        >>> ctx = RoutingContext(task_type="code", estimated_tokens=2000)
59lz|        >>> ctx.environment
60id|        'production'
61rt|    """
62nd|
63ke|    task_type: str | None = None
64uj|    estimated_tokens: int = 0
65ck|    environment: str = "production"
66nw|    latency_budget_ms: int | None = None
67th|
68th|
69vg|class RoutingRule(BaseModel):
70au|    """A single routing rule that maps a condition to a backend.
71st|
72ov|    Attributes:
73ja|        condition: A callable that accepts a :class:`RoutingContext` and
74ya|            returns ``True`` if this rule should fire.
75rd|        backend_name: Key in the ``backends`` dict to use when this rule fires.
76ik|        priority: Evaluation order — lower numbers are checked first.
77th|
78ow|    Example:
79lb|        >>> rule = RoutingRule(
80ub|        ...     condition=lambda ctx: ctx.task_type == "summary",
81wo|        ...     backend_name="anthropic",
82pr|        ...     priority=5,
83bz|        ... )
84ri|    """
85th|
86ie|    condition: Callable[[RoutingContext], bool]
87lz|    backend_name: str
88lm|    priority: int
89th|
90ou|    model_config = {"arbitrary_types_allowed": True}
91st|
92nd|
93ru|class LLMRouter:
94bz|    """Routes LLM calls to different backends based on priority-ordered rules.
95th|
96vc|    On each call, :meth:`route` evaluates the registered rules in ascending
97jt|    ``priority`` order and returns the backend of the first matching rule.
98cq|    If no rule matches, the ``default_backend`` is used.
99th|
100zn|    Args:
101eq|        rules: List of routing rules to evaluate.
102ti|        backends: Mapping of backend name to :class:`BaseLLMBackend` instance.
103mq|        default_backend: Name of the fallback backend when no rule matches.
104bv|            Defaults to the first key in ``backends`` if not specified.
105th|
106ji|    Raises:
107oy|        ConfigurationError: If no backends are provided, or if ``route`` is
108ys|            called but no matching backend exists.
109th|
110ow|    Example:
111ly|        >>> from cdl_llm.gateway.router import LLMRouter, RoutingContext, RoutingRule
112ve|        >>> router = LLMRouter(
113bc|        ...     rules=[RoutingRule(
114jm|        ...         condition=lambda ctx: ctx.task_type == "qa",
115of|        ...         backend_name="openai",
116je|        ...         priority=1,
117sn|        ...     )],
118cr|        ...     backends={"openai": openai_llm},
119wt|        ...     default_backend="openai",
120ep|        ... )
121ah|    """
122nd|
123fs|    def __init__(
124ss|        self,
125lm|        rules: list[RoutingRule],
126iu|        backends: dict[str, BaseLLMBackend],
127sx|        default_backend: str | None = None,
128px|    ) -> None:
129rf|        if not backends:
130hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
131fj|                "LLMRouter requires at least one backend in the 'backends' dict."
132tg|            )
133gd|        self._rules: list[RoutingRule] = sorted(rules, key=lambda r: r.priority)
134fa|        self._backends: dict[str, BaseLLMBackend] = backends
135op|        self._default: str = default_backend or next(iter(backends))
136th|
137pp|    def route(self, context: RoutingContext) -> BaseLLMBackend:
138ja|        """Select a backend for the given routing context.
139th|
140dj|        Evaluates rules in ascending ``priority`` order and returns the backend
141gl|        of the first matching rule.  Falls back to ``default_backend`` if no
142fk|        rule matches.
143rd|
144oz|        Args:
145zd|            context: Metadata describing the current request.
146th|
147rt|        Returns:
148bw|            The selected :class:`BaseLLMBackend` instance.
149th|
150nu|        Raises:
151ji|            ConfigurationError: If the selected backend name is not in
152ab|                ``backends``, or if the default backend is not registered.
153rd|
154sn|        Example:
155dk|            >>> ctx = RoutingContext(task_type="code")
156jw|            >>> backend = router.route(ctx)
157nm|        """
158dy|        for rule in self._rules:
159fj|            if rule.condition(context):
160uk|                backend = self._backends.get(rule.backend_name)
161fv|                if backend is None:
162vl|                    raise ConfigurationError(
163dj|                        f"RoutingRule references backend '{rule.backend_name}' "
164te|                        "which is not registered in the router's backends dict."
165za|                    )
166si|                return backend
167th|
168jr|        # No rule matched — use default
169ri|        backend = self._backends.get(self._default)
170tk|        if backend is None:
171hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
172ip|                f"Default backend '{self._default}' is not registered in "
173mx|                "the router's backends dict."
174wj|            )
175sa|        return backend
176th|
177tg|    def add_rule(self, rule: RoutingRule) -> None:
178hu|        """Add a new routing rule and re-sort by priority.
179th|
180oz|        Args:
181ew|            rule: The :class:`RoutingRule` to register.
182nd|
183sn|        Example:
184ov|            >>> router.add_rule(RoutingRule(
185cn|            ...     condition=lambda ctx: ctx.estimated_tokens > 10000,
186zo|            ...     backend_name="anthropic",
187le|            ...     priority=2,
188ov|            ... ))
189kd|        """
190es|        self._rules.append(rule)
191bg|        self._rules.sort(key=lambda r: r.priority)
192nd|
193dm|    def invoke(
194ss|        self,
195ej|        messages: list[BaseMessage],
196do|        context: RoutingContext,
197xt|        **kwargs: Any,
198nk|    ) -> AIMessage:
199qe|        """Route and invoke synchronously.
200th|
201wh|        Selects a backend via :meth:`route` and calls its :meth:`invoke`.
202nd|
203oz|        Args:
204tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
205is|            context: Metadata used to select the appropriate backend.
206nm|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the backend's invoke.
207th|
208rt|        Returns:
209jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
210th|
211nu|        Raises:
212qx|            ConfigurationError: If no suitable backend can be resolved.
213iu|            BackendError: If the selected backend's invoke call fails.
214th|
215sn|        Example:
216hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
217kf|            >>> ctx = RoutingContext(task_type="qa")
218no|            >>> response = router.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")], ctx)
219pm|        """
220hm|        backend = self.route(context)
221tl|        return backend.invoke(messages, **kwargs)
222nd|
223ri|    async def ainvoke(
224ss|        self,
225ej|        messages: list[BaseMessage],
226do|        context: RoutingContext,
227xt|        **kwargs: Any,
228nk|    ) -> AIMessage:
229qy|        """Route and invoke asynchronously.
230th|
231qy|        Selects a backend via :meth:`route` and calls its :meth:`ainvoke`.
232nd|
233oz|        Args:
234tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
235is|            context: Metadata used to select the appropriate backend.
236fm|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the backend's ainvoke.
237th|
238rt|        Returns:
239jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
240th|
241nu|        Raises:
242qx|            ConfigurationError: If no suitable backend can be resolved.
243hj|            BackendError: If the selected backend's ainvoke call fails.
244th|
245sn|        Example:
246hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
247kf|            >>> ctx = RoutingContext(task_type="qa")
248gu|            >>> response = await router.ainvoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")], ctx)
249ne|        """
250hm|        backend = self.route(context)
251ww|        return await backend.ainvoke(messages, **kwargs)
252nd|

1ah|"""
2zu|Abstract base class for all LangGraph template implementations.
3rd|
4ck|All graph templates inherit from BaseGraphTemplate. Subclasses define
5wi|nodes and edges either via the legacy ``build()`` pattern or the new
6ta|validation-aware pattern using ``_define_nodes()`` and ``_define_edges()``.
7th|
8ki|**Validation-aware pattern (recommended):**
9al|Subclasses that override ``_define_nodes()`` and ``_define_edges()`` benefit
10yi|from automatic structural validation at ``compile()`` time. Misconfigured
11ze|graphs fail at service startup with a clear list of all errors rather than
12cx|a cryptic runtime exception during live traffic.
13th|
14rt|Design inspired by Haystack's pipeline validation pattern.
15th|
16ch|Example (new pattern)::
17th|
18wp|    class MyGraph(BaseGraphTemplate):
19vs|        _state_class = MyState
20th|
21rc|        @property
22sg|        def _entry_node(self) -> str:
23wb|            return "retrieve"
24th|
25vc|        def _define_nodes(self) -> None:
26iy|            self._add_node("retrieve", self._retrieve)
27lc|            self._add_node("generate", self._generate)
28th|
29kx|        def _define_edges(self) -> None:
30sf|            self._add_edge("retrieve", "generate")
31ul|            self._add_edge("generate", END)
32nd|
33sr|Example (legacy ``build()`` pattern)::
34th|
35wp|    class MyGraph(BaseGraphTemplate):
36nh|        def build(self) -> StateGraph:
37ys|            g = StateGraph(PipelineState)
38vw|            g.add_node("process", self._process)
39dv|            g.set_entry_point("process")
40tz|            g.add_edge("process", END)
41wa|            return g
42wn|"""
43rd|
44kt|from __future__ import annotations
45th|
46rw|from typing import Any, Callable, TypedDict, TYPE_CHECKING
47th|
48hl|from pydantic import BaseModel
49th|
50hq|from cdl_llm.exceptions import GraphValidationError
51st|
52wy|from .state import BaseGraphState
53rd|
54zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
55ij|    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
56iy|    from langgraph.graph.graph import CompiledGraph
57th|
58fd|from ..llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
59th|
60th|
61rv|class EdgeDefinition(TypedDict):
62uy|    """A directed edge between two graph nodes."""
63rd|
64my|    source: str
65kd|    target: str
66gt|    condition: str | None  # None = unconditional edge
67th|
68th|
69lz|class BaseGraphTemplate:
70ob|    """Abstract template for building and compiling LangGraph state graphs.
71st|
72tu|    Subclasses may implement either the legacy ``build()`` pattern or the
73of|    new validation-aware pattern using ``_define_nodes()`` and ``_define_edges()``.
74th|
75ux|    **Validation-aware pattern**: override ``_define_nodes()``, ``_define_edges()``,
76je|    and the ``_entry_node`` property. Set ``_state_class`` as a class variable.
77xm|    The ``compile()`` method runs :meth:`validate` automatically before building.
78th|
79cs|    **Legacy pattern**: override ``build()`` directly. No validation is performed.
80th|
81zn|    Args:
82vy|        llm: LLM backend injected into graph nodes. Optional for graphs that
83hr|            use the new ``_define_nodes()`` pattern without an LLM.
84wk|        config: Optional configuration dict for graph-specific settings.
85th|
86ow|    Example:
87ik|        >>> template = MyGraphTemplate(llm=llm)
88gy|        >>> graph = template.compile()
89qm|        >>> result = await graph.ainvoke({"query": "Hello"})
90uy|    """
91st|
92aw|    # Class variable: override to set the LangGraph state type for _build_graph()
93ma|    _state_class: type = BaseGraphState
94th|
95fs|    def __init__(
96ss|        self,
97bu|        llm: BaseLLMBackend | None = None,
98hu|        config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
99px|    ) -> None:
100uq|        self._llm = llm
101dd|        self._config = config or {}
102ih|        # Internal node/edge registry for the validation-aware pattern
103fz|        self._nodes: dict[str, Callable[..., Any]] = {}
104nk|        self._edges: list[EdgeDefinition] = []
105bk|        self._conditional_edges: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
106th|
107hi|    # ── New validation-aware pattern ─────────────────────────────────────────
108th|
109jq|    @property
110db|    def _entry_node(self) -> str:
111nv|        """Name of the node that receives the initial state.
112th|
113nx|        Override in subclasses that use the ``_define_nodes()`` pattern.
114fa|        """
115ks|        return ""
116th|
117wr|    def _define_nodes(self) -> None:
118tu|        """Register all nodes with :meth:`_add_node`.
119th|
120ll|        Override this method in subclasses that use the validation-aware
121wx|        pattern. Called automatically by :meth:`validate` and :meth:`compile`.
122gv|        """
123rd|
124it|    def _define_edges(self) -> None:
125uv|        """Register all edges with :meth:`_add_edge` or :meth:`_add_conditional_edge`.
126th|
127ll|        Override this method in subclasses that use the validation-aware
128wx|        pattern. Called automatically by :meth:`validate` and :meth:`compile`.
129vq|        """
130th|
131uw|    def _add_node(self, name: str, fn: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
132gm|        """Register a node. Call from :meth:`_define_nodes`.
133rd|
134oz|        Args:
135ge|            name: Unique node name. Referenced by edges.
136cu|            fn: Callable that receives and returns the graph state.
137ln|        """
138xz|        self._nodes[name] = fn
139th|
140dm|    def _add_edge(self, source: str, target: str) -> None:
141ft|        """Register an unconditional directed edge. Call from :meth:`_define_edges`.
142nd|
143oz|        Args:
144rz|            source: Name of the source node.
145uv|            target: Name of the target node, or ``END``.
146uq|        """
147hq|        self._edges.append(EdgeDefinition(source=source, target=target, condition=None))
148th|
149uz|    def _add_conditional_edge(
150ss|        self,
151me|        source: str,
152mw|        condition_fn: Callable[..., str],
153wm|        target_map: dict[str, str],
154px|    ) -> None:
155ej|        """Register a conditional edge. Call from :meth:`_define_edges`.
156th|
157oz|        Args:
158rz|            source: Name of the source node.
159th|            condition_fn: Callable that returns a key from ``target_map``.
160ne|            target_map: Mapping from condition return value to target node name.
161vl|        """
162gm|        self._conditional_edges.append(
163ji|            {"source": source, "fn": condition_fn, "map": target_map}
164zz|        )
165th|
166wp|    def validate(self) -> list[str]:
167pl|        """Validate the graph structure. Returns all errors found.
168th|
169xi|        Resets the node and edge registry, calls ``_define_nodes()`` and
170ap|        ``_define_edges()``, then checks for structural problems.
171st|
172rc|        Checks performed:
173rd|
174wl|        1. Entry node is registered in ``_nodes``
175ui|        2. All edge sources and targets exist in ``_nodes`` or are ``END``
176nb|        3. No nodes are unreachable from the entry node
177ji|        4. Entry node has no incoming edges (it is a true entry point)
178jp|        5. At least one edge leads to ``END``
179th|
180rt|        Returns:
181qc|            list[str]: Human-readable error descriptions. Empty list = valid.
182rq|        """
183gp|        from langgraph.graph import END
184th|
185ut|        # Reset to avoid double-registration on repeated validate() calls
186is|        self._nodes = {}
187ul|        self._edges = []
188yk|        self._conditional_edges = []
189th|
190im|        self._define_nodes()
191vh|        self._define_edges()
192nd|
193jo|        errors: list[str] = []
194jl|        declared = set(self._nodes.keys())
195fi|        valid_targets = declared | {END}
196th|
197tm|        # Check 1: entry node exists
198at|        if self._entry_node not in declared:
199ra|            errors.append(
200tx|                f"Entry node '{self._entry_node}' is not registered. "
201zk|                f"Register it with self._add_node('{self._entry_node}', ...)"
202pr|            )
203rd|
204fy|        # Check 2: all edge endpoints exist
205ym|        for edge in self._edges:
206tk|            if edge["source"] not in declared:
207xz|                errors.append(
208oz|                    f"Edge source '{edge['source']}' is not a registered node. "
209hb|                    f"Registered nodes: {sorted(declared)}"
210db|                )
211aq|            if edge["target"] not in valid_targets:
212xz|                errors.append(
213gm|                    f"Edge target '{edge['target']}' is not a registered node or END. "
214uv|                    f"Valid targets: {sorted(valid_targets)}"
215cj|                )
216th|
217fy|        # Check 3: reachability from entry
218tj|        if self._entry_node in declared:
219sm|            reachable = self._get_reachable(self._entry_node)
220za|            unreachable = declared - reachable
221ip|            for node in sorted(unreachable):
222xz|                errors.append(
223gd|                    f"Node '{node}' is unreachable from entry node "
224wh|                    f"'{self._entry_node}'. Add an edge leading to it or remove it."
225xs|                )
226th|
227dg|        # Check 4: entry node has no incoming edges
228fu|        incoming_to_entry = [e for e in self._edges if e["target"] == self._entry_node]
229dl|        if incoming_to_entry:
230ra|            errors.append(
231kv|                f"Entry node '{self._entry_node}' has incoming edges from: "
232hv|                f"{[e['source'] for e in incoming_to_entry]}. "
233lr|                "Entry nodes must have no incoming edges."
234cg|            )
235th|
236iq|        # Check 5: at least one path to END
237yu|        end_targets = [e for e in self._edges if e["target"] == END]
238fg|        if not end_targets:
239ra|            errors.append(
240oc|                "No edge leads to END. The graph will never terminate. "
241tv|                "Add self._add_edge('<last_node>', END) in _define_edges()."
242gm|            )
243rd|
244tv|        return errors
245th|
246vk|    def _get_reachable(self, start: str) -> set[str]:
247jt|        """BFS from ``start`` node. Returns all reachable node names.
248th|
249oz|        Args:
250fh|            start: Name of the starting node.
251st|
252rt|        Returns:
253nl|            Set of node names reachable from ``start`` via registered edges.
254mb|        """
255as|        visited: set[str] = set()
256zg|        queue = [start]
257dg|        while queue:
258ca|            node = queue.pop(0)
259ke|            if node in visited:
260yt|                continue
261at|            visited.add(node)
262rs|            neighbors = [e["target"] for e in self._edges if e["source"] == node]
263ju|            queue.extend(n for n in neighbors if n not in visited and n in self._nodes)
264ar|        return visited
265th|
266pu|    def _build_graph(self) -> CompiledGraph:
267by|        """Build and compile the LangGraph StateGraph after validation passes.
268th|
269cb|        Uses the nodes and edges registered by ``_define_nodes()`` and
270fp|        ``_define_edges()``. Called automatically by :meth:`compile` when
271ex|        the validation-aware pattern is detected.
272nd|
273rt|        Returns:
274hk|            A compiled, invokable LangGraph.
275yj|        """
276pd|        from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph
277th|
278ff|        graph: StateGraph = StateGraph(self._state_class)
279ot|        for name, fn in self._nodes.items():
280nw|            graph.add_node(name, fn)
281oy|        graph.set_entry_point(self._entry_node)
282ym|        for edge in self._edges:
283xf|            if edge["target"] == END:
284fh|                graph.add_edge(edge["source"], END)
285iu|            else:
286ss|                graph.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])
287fi|        for cond in self._conditional_edges:
288of|            graph.add_conditional_edges(cond["source"], cond["fn"], cond["map"])
289ng|        return graph.compile()
290th|
291gt|    # ── Legacy build() pattern ────────────────────────────────────────────────
292nd|
293bx|    def build(self) -> StateGraph:
294dj|        """Construct and return the :class:`StateGraph` (uncompiled).
295th|
296vx|        Override in subclasses that use the legacy ``build()`` pattern.
297ji|        Subclasses using the new ``_define_nodes()`` / ``_define_edges()``
298qo|        pattern do not need to override this method.
299th|
300rt|        Returns:
301av|            A fully configured :class:`StateGraph` with all nodes and edges.
302nd|
303nu|        Raises:
304bm|            NotImplementedError: If neither ``build()`` nor ``_define_nodes()``
305nh|                is overridden by the subclass.
306vi|        """
307ju|        raise NotImplementedError(
308ts|            f"{type(self).__name__} must implement either build() or _define_nodes()."
309sh|        )
310th|
311ny|    def compile(self, checkpointer: Any = None) -> CompiledGraph:
312ne|        """Validate and compile the graph to a runnable LangGraph.
313th|
314yd|        If the subclass overrides ``_define_nodes()``, the validation-aware
315xf|        path is used: :meth:`validate` runs first, and any structural errors
316ox|        raise :class:`~cdl_llm.exceptions.GraphValidationError` with all
317ff|        issues listed.
318th|
319vt|        If the subclass only overrides ``build()``, the legacy path is used
320ra|        and no structural validation is performed.
321st|
322oz|        Args:
323hx|            checkpointer: Optional LangGraph checkpointer for state persistence
324ct|                and Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) support. Only applicable to the
325ky|                legacy ``build()`` path.
326th|
327rt|        Returns:
328hk|            A compiled, invokable LangGraph.
329th|
330nu|        Raises:
331ak|            GraphValidationError: If :meth:`validate` returns any errors.
332sl|                Contains all errors — fix them all before retrying.
333cg|        """
334fn|        # Detect which pattern the subclass uses.
335ud|        # If _define_nodes is overridden, use the new validation-aware path.
336tm|        uses_new_pattern = (
337fb|            type(self)._define_nodes is not BaseGraphTemplate._define_nodes
338pl|        )
339th|
340ig|        if uses_new_pattern:
341en|            errors = self.validate()
342wy|            if errors:
343xn|                raise GraphValidationError(errors=errors)
344fy|            return self._build_graph()
345th|
346wn|        # Legacy path: subclass implements build()
347jk|        return self.build().compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
348th|
349mq|    # ── Schema declarations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
350th|
351cu|    def get_input_schema(self) -> type[BaseModel]:
352lo|        """Return the Pydantic model class describing the expected input.
353rd|
354lw|        Override in subclasses to declare the expected input shape for
355vr|        documentation and validation.
356th|
357rt|        Returns:
358ur|            A :class:`pydantic.BaseModel` subclass, or :class:`BaseModel`
359hx|            as the default.
360fn|        """
361mg|        return BaseModel
362nd|
363ez|    def get_output_schema(self) -> type[BaseModel]:
364kb|        """Return the Pydantic model class describing the expected output.
365th|
366wa|        Override in subclasses to declare the expected output shape for
367vr|        documentation and validation.
368th|
369rt|        Returns:
370ur|            A :class:`pydantic.BaseModel` subclass, or :class:`BaseModel`
371hx|            as the default.
372xb|        """
373mg|        return BaseModel
374th|

1ah|"""
2zu|Abstract base class for all evaluators.
3rd|
4cp|All evaluator implementations must inherit from :class:`BaseEvaluator` and
5no|implement the ``evaluate`` method.
6th|
7dl|Example::
8th|
9bn|    class MyEvaluator(BaseEvaluator[str]):
10xh|        def evaluate(self, prediction, reference, context=None):
11zg|            score = 1.0 if prediction == reference else 0.0
12oh|            return EvalResult(
13mf|                metric="my_metric", score=score,
14xf|                passed=score >= 0.8, threshold=0.8,
15ob|            )
16cv|"""
17th|
18kt|from __future__ import annotations
19th|
20ci|import abc
21bj|from typing import Generic, TypeVar
22nd|
23ib|from ..types import EvalResult
24th|
25tf|T = TypeVar("T")
26th|
27th|
28jp|class BaseEvaluator(abc.ABC, Generic[T]):
29tb|    """Abstract evaluator interface.
30th|
31lx|    Generic over the prediction/reference type ``T``.  Most text evaluators
32lt|    use ``T = str``.
33rd|
34ow|    Example:
35ax|        >>> class ExactMatch(BaseEvaluator[str]):
36dm|        ...     def evaluate(self, prediction, reference, context=None):
37av|        ...         score = 1.0 if prediction.strip() == reference.strip() else 0.0
38bj|        ...         return EvalResult(metric="exact_match", score=score,
39nl|        ...                           passed=score == 1.0, threshold=1.0)
40bv|    """
41st|
42bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
43eg|    def evaluate(
44ss|        self,
45ix|        prediction: T,
46rz|        reference: T,
47jt|        context: str | None = None,
48gf|    ) -> EvalResult:
49hv|        """Evaluate a single prediction against a reference.
50th|
51oz|        Args:
52qp|            prediction: The model-generated output to evaluate.
53or|            reference: The ground-truth reference output.
54id|            context: Optional context string (e.g. retrieved documents for
55jl|                faithfulness evaluation).
56th|
57rt|        Returns:
58db|            An :class:`EvalResult` with the metric name, score, and pass/fail.
59hf|        """
60th|
61cj|    async def aevaluate(
62ss|        self,
63ix|        prediction: T,
64rz|        reference: T,
65jt|        context: str | None = None,
66gf|    ) -> EvalResult:
67jm|        """Async version of :meth:`evaluate`.
68th|
69zf|        Default implementation runs :meth:`evaluate` in a thread executor.
70th|
71oz|        Args:
72qp|            prediction: The model-generated output to evaluate.
73or|            reference: The ground-truth reference output.
74dr|            context: Optional context string.
75th|
76rt|        Returns:
77db|            An :class:`EvalResult` with the metric name, score, and pass/fail.
78uz|        """
79my|        import asyncio
80th|
81wx|        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
82uy|        return await loop.run_in_executor(
83ja|            None, lambda: self.evaluate(prediction, reference, context)
84eq|        )
85th|

1ah|"""
