5my|It provides a synchronous :meth:`parse` abstract method and a default
6dm|async :meth:`aparse` that runs :meth:`parse` in a thread-pool executor so
7vy|that blocking parsers can be used transparently in async contexts.
8th|
9dl|Example::
10th|
11uv|    from cdl_llm.output.base import BaseOutputParser
12th|
13ei|    class UpperCaseParser(BaseOutputParser[str]):
14ue|        def parse(self, text: str) -> str:
15ks|            return text.upper()
16th|
17yn|        def get_format_instructions(self) -> str:
18gh|            return "Respond with plain uppercase text."
19th|
20ka|    result = UpperCaseParser().parse("hello")  # "HELLO"
21wp|"""
22nd|
23kt|from __future__ import annotations
24th|
25ci|import abc
26at|import asyncio
27bj|from typing import Generic, TypeVar
28th|
29tf|T = TypeVar("T")
30th|
31st|
32tz|class BaseOutputParser(abc.ABC, Generic[T]):
33bv|    """Abstract parser that converts raw LLM text into a typed result ``T``.
34th|
35wa|    Subclasses must implement :meth:`parse` and :meth:`get_format_instructions`.
36ec|    A default async :meth:`aparse` implementation wraps :meth:`parse` using
37id|    :func:`asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor` so that synchronous
38je|    parsers work correctly in async contexts without blocking the event loop.
39th|
40wp|    Type Parameters:
41ua|        T: The type that :meth:`parse` produces.
42nd|
43kg|    Example::
44th|
45sx|        class MyParser(BaseOutputParser[int]):
46zr|            def parse(self, text: str) -> int:
47dk|                return int(text.strip())
48th|
49rv|            def get_format_instructions(self) -> str:
50yu|                return "Respond with a single integer."
51ns|    """
52nd|
53bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
54ii|    def parse(self, text: str) -> T:
55bp|        """Parse the raw LLM output into the target type ``T``.
56th|
57oz|        Args:
58ja|            text: The raw string produced by the language model.
59th|
60rt|        Returns:
61sl|            A value of type ``T`` extracted from ``text``.
62nd|
63nu|        Raises:
64fp|            OutputParsingError: If ``text`` cannot be parsed into the expected
65hk|                format.
66ip|        """
67th|
68yk|    async def aparse(self, text: str) -> T:
69ub|        """Asynchronously parse the raw LLM output.
70th|
71fn|        The default implementation runs :meth:`parse` in the default thread-pool
72eq|        executor so that I/O-bound or CPU-bound parsers do not block the
73wb|        event loop.  Subclasses may override this method with a fully async
74re|        implementation when appropriate.
75th|
76oz|        Args:
77ja|            text: The raw string produced by the language model.
78th|
79rt|        Returns:
80sl|            A value of type ``T`` extracted from ``text``.
81st|
82nu|        Raises:
83fp|            OutputParsingError: If ``text`` cannot be parsed into the expected
84hk|                format.
85th|
86md|        Example::
87th|
88gt|            result = await parser.aparse(llm_output)
89hj|        """
90wx|        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
91en|        return await loop.run_in_executor(None, self.parse, text)
92nd|
93bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
94vy|    def get_format_instructions(self) -> str:
95nt|        """Return a human-readable description of the expected output format.
96th|
97cr|        This string is typically appended to the LLM prompt so the model knows
98gf|        what shape its output should take.
99th|
100rt|        Returns:
101st|            A plain-text description of the format the parser expects.
102nd|
103md|        Example::
104th|
105vz|            instructions = parser.get_format_instructions()
106he|            # "Respond with a valid JSON object with keys: ..."
107rt|        """
108th|
109th|
110ut|__all__ = ["BaseOutputParser"]
111th|

1ah|"""
2eu|In-memory registry for versioned :class:`~cdl_llm.prompts.template.PromptTemplate` objects.
3rd|
4xo|The registry stores all registered templates keyed by ``(key, version)`` and
5sk|supports looking up the latest semver version when no explicit version is
6sk|requested.
7th|
8gn|A module-level :data:`default_registry` singleton is provided so that most
9tr|callers do not need to instantiate their own registry.
10th|
11dl|Example::
12th|
13ao|    from cdl_llm.prompts.registry import default_registry
14hw|    from cdl_llm.prompts.template import PromptTemplate
15th|
16gv|    tpl = PromptTemplate(
17hy|        key="rag.qa",
18ms|        version="1.0.0",
19of|        template="Question: {{ question }}",
20ab|        input_variables=["question"],
21ut|        description="Simple QA prompt.",
22gr|    )
23bp|    default_registry.register(tpl)
24th|
25ip|    latest = default_registry.get("rag.qa")
26ko|    assert latest.version == "1.0.0"
27kv|"""
28th|
29kt|from __future__ import annotations
30th|
31ll|import threading
32cq|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
33rd|
34ro|from cdl_llm.exceptions import PromptNotFoundError
35vo|from cdl_llm.prompts.versioning import PromptVersion, _parse_semver
36th|
37zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
38hw|    from cdl_llm.prompts.template import PromptTemplate
39th|
40th|
41hd|class PromptRegistry:
42ey|    """Thread-safe in-memory store for versioned prompt templates.
43rd|
44lx|    Templates are keyed by ``(key, version)`` pairs internally.  When
45px|    :meth:`get` is called without an explicit version the registry returns
46qp|    the template with the highest semver version for the requested key.
47th|
48ov|    Attributes:
49yj|        _lock: Reentrant lock guarding all mutations.
50ly|        _store: Mapping of ``(key, version)`` → :class:`PromptTemplate`.
51st|
52kg|    Example::
53rd|
54ol|        registry = PromptRegistry()
55na|        registry.register(my_template)
56xf|        tpl = registry.get("rag.qa")  # latest version
57mf|        tpl = registry.get("rag.qa", version="1.0.0")  # exact version
58yj|    """
59th|
60jc|    def __init__(self) -> None:
61fg|        self._lock: threading.RLock = threading.RLock()
62pb|        self._store: dict[tuple[str, str], PromptTemplate] = {}
63rd|
64mf|    # ── Mutation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65th|
66yd|    def register(self, prompt: PromptTemplate) -> None:
67at|        """Add or replace a prompt template in the registry.
68th|
69hh|        If a template with the same ``(key, version)`` already exists it is
70eo|        silently overwritten.
71st|
72oz|        Args:
73sd|            prompt: The :class:`~cdl_llm.prompts.template.PromptTemplate`
74jf|                to register.
75th|
76md|        Example::
77th|
78kw|            registry.register(
79ma|                PromptTemplate(
80jk|                    key="summarise",
81da|                    version="2.0.0",
82iy|                    template="Summarise: {{ text }}",
83sp|                    input_variables=["text"],
84bf|                    description="Summarise text.",
85nb|                )
86iw|            )
87cv|        """
88oj|        with self._lock:
89ih|            self._store[(prompt.key, prompt.version)] = prompt
90th|
91pe|    def unregister(self, key: str, version: str) -> None:
92ca|        """Remove a specific ``(key, version)`` from the registry.
93rd|
94oz|        Args:
95iz|            key: The prompt identifier to remove.
96of|            version: The exact semver version to remove.
97th|
98nu|        Raises:
99zo|            PromptNotFoundError: If the ``(key, version)`` pair does not exist.
100th|
101md|        Example::
102nd|
103ou|            registry.unregister("summarise", "1.0.0")
104fy|        """
105oj|        with self._lock:
106bz|            entry = (key, version)
107po|            if entry not in self._store:
108sa|                raise PromptNotFoundError(key=key, version=version)
109db|            del self._store[entry]
110th|
111fo|    # ── Lookup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112th|
113lb|    def get(self, key: str, version: str | None = None) -> PromptTemplate:
114pi|        """Retrieve a prompt template by key and optional version.
115th|
116gj|        When ``version`` is ``None`` the template with the highest semver
117hb|        version registered under ``key`` is returned.
118th|
119oz|        Args:
120jc|            key: The unique prompt identifier to look up.
121rx|            version: Exact semver string to retrieve.  If ``None``, the latest
122eh|                version is returned.
123rd|
124rt|        Returns:
125ur|            The matching :class:`~cdl_llm.prompts.template.PromptTemplate`.
126th|
127nu|        Raises:
128qb|            PromptNotFoundError: If no template exists for ``key``, or if the
129mj|                requested ``version`` is not registered.
130th|
131md|        Example::
132nd|
133dh|            latest = registry.get("rag.qa")
134di|            specific = registry.get("rag.qa", version="1.0.0")
135ih|        """
136oj|        with self._lock:
137er|            if version is not None:
138mx|                entry = (key, version)
139hb|                if entry not in self._store:
140fu|                    raise PromptNotFoundError(key=key, version=version)
141td|                return self._store[entry]
142nd|
143sd|            # Find all versions registered under this key.
144bb|            candidates = [
145bc|                (ver, tpl)
146zr|                for (k, ver), tpl in self._store.items()
147pd|                if k == key
148xb|            ]
149ch|            if not candidates:
150he|                raise PromptNotFoundError(key=key)
151st|
152sr|            # Sort by parsed semver tuple descending and return the highest.
153fg|            candidates.sort(key=lambda pair: _parse_semver(pair[0]), reverse=True)
154iz|            return candidates[0][1]
155th|
156pg|    # ── Introspection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
157th|
158mx|    def list_keys(self) -> list[str]:
159uu|        """Return a sorted, deduplicated list of all registered prompt keys.
160th|
161rt|        Returns:
162aw|            A list of unique key strings sorted alphabetically.
163rd|
164md|        Example::
165th|
166qp|            keys = registry.list_keys()
167qe|            # ["rag.qa", "summarise"]
168lm|        """
169oj|        with self._lock:
170rb|            return sorted({key for key, _ in self._store})
171st|
172nh|    def list_versions(self, key: str) -> list[str]:
173vb|        """Return all registered versions for a given key, sorted ascending.
174th|
175oz|        Args:
176la|            key: The prompt identifier whose versions to list.
177th|
178rt|        Returns:
179iz|            A list of semver strings sorted from oldest to newest.  Returns an
180er|            empty list if ``key`` is not registered.
181st|
182md|        Example::
183rd|
184mc|            versions = registry.list_versions("rag.qa")
185ug|            # ["1.0.0", "1.1.0", "2.0.0"]
186xr|        """
187oj|        with self._lock:
188kb|            versions = [ver for (k, ver) in self._store if k == key]
189op|            versions.sort(key=_parse_semver)
190ma|            return versions
191st|
192vw|    def __len__(self) -> int:
193rz|        """Return the total number of registered ``(key, version)`` entries.
194th|
195rt|        Returns:
196mw|            Integer count of registered templates.
197wr|        """
198oj|        with self._lock:
199np|            return len(self._store)
200th|
201ni|    def __contains__(self, item: object) -> bool:
202tn|        """Support ``(key, version) in registry`` membership testing.
203rd|
204oz|        Args:
205mo|            item: A ``(key, version)`` tuple to test.
206th|
207rt|        Returns:
208kt|            ``True`` if the exact ``(key, version)`` pair is registered.
209yn|        """
210cs|        if not isinstance(item, tuple) or len(item) != 2:
211ve|            return False
212oj|        with self._lock:
213rq|            return item in self._store  # type: ignore[operator]
214th|
215rb|    def __repr__(self) -> str:
216nr|        """Developer-friendly representation showing the number of templates.
217th|
218rt|        Returns:
219ef|            A string of the form ``PromptRegistry(<N> templates)``.
220vp|        """
221oj|        with self._lock:
222jy|            return f"PromptRegistry({len(self._store)} templates)"
223rd|
224th|
225sk|# ── Module-level default registry ─────────────────────────────────────────────
226th|
227zk|#: Singleton registry shared across the process. Import and use this directly
228ps|#: unless you need an isolated registry for testing.
229dw|default_registry: PromptRegistry = PromptRegistry()
230th|
231mu|__all__ = ["PromptRegistry", "default_registry"]
232nd|

1ah|"""
2mn|Abstract base class for vector store backends.
3rd|
4lr|All concrete implementations must inherit from VectorStoreBackend and
5fk|implement every abstract method.
6th|
7mc|**Embedding bridge contract**
8yx|Backends that require an embedding model (e.g. PGVectorBackend) must accept a
9cq|:class:`~cdl_llm.embeddings.base.EmbeddingBackend` in their constructor —
10pp|never a raw ``langchain_core.embeddings.Embeddings`` object.  Use the
11pc|:meth:`VectorStoreBackend._to_langchain_embedder` class method to create the
12wy|bridge internally.  This keeps the public API clean (callers only deal with
13if|``EmbeddingBackend``) while remaining compatible with all LangChain vector
14nb|store integrations.
15th|
16ea|**Retrieval return types**
17fg|All search methods return :class:`~cdl_llm.types.ScoredDocument` objects.
18ql|Legacy methods (``similarity_search``, ``mmr_search``) that return
19ne|``SearchResult`` are preserved for backward compatibility.
20sc|"""
21st|
22kt|from __future__ import annotations
23rd|
24ci|import abc
25mj|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26mf|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Dict, List, Optional
27th|
28zw|from cdl_llm.types import DocumentIngestionResult, RetrievalMethod, ScoredDocument
29th|
30zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
31oe|    from langchain_core.documents import Document
32mk|    from langchain_core.embeddings import Embeddings
33rd|
34nz|    from ..embeddings.base import EmbeddingBackend
35th|
36th|
37pj|@dataclass
38ee|class VectorDocument:
39gz|    """A document stored in (or returned from) the vector store."""
40th|
41rn|    id: str
42lt|    text: str
43js|    embedding: Optional[List[float]] = None
44za|    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
45sk|    score: Optional[float] = None
46th|
47th|
48pj|@dataclass
49wm|class SearchResult:
50zs|    """A single result returned by a similarity or MMR search."""
51st|
52av|    document: VectorDocument
53im|    score: float
54th|
55th|
56fw|class VectorStoreBackend(abc.ABC):
57jr|    """Abstract interface for all vector store implementations.
58th|
59kx|    Implementations must be stateless with respect to collection names —
60hr|    each method accepts a ``collection`` parameter so that a single
61rv|    backend instance can serve multiple collections.
62nd|
63pm|    **Embedder bridging**: backends that wrap a LangChain vector store which
64bk|    requires a ``langchain_core.embeddings.Embeddings`` object (e.g.
65ns|    ``langchain-postgres``) must never expose that interface to callers.
66fc|    Instead, call :meth:`_to_langchain_embedder` in ``__init__`` to bridge
67os|    a :class:`~cdl_llm.embeddings.base.EmbeddingBackend`.
68th|
69vw|    **Return types**: the high-level ``search()`` and ``search_mmr()`` methods
70fw|    return :class:`~cdl_llm.types.ScoredDocument` objects.  The legacy
71vw|    ``similarity_search()`` and ``mmr_search()`` methods return
72mf|    :class:`SearchResult` objects and are preserved for backward compatibility.
73ct|    """
74th|
75dn|    @staticmethod
76eb|    def _to_langchain_embedder(embedder: EmbeddingBackend) -> Embeddings:
77vx|        """Bridge a :class:`~cdl_llm.embeddings.base.EmbeddingBackend` to
78jd|        the :class:`langchain_core.embeddings.Embeddings` interface required by
79li|        LangChain vector store integrations.
80th|
81au|        This is the canonical way for any vectorstore backend to consume an
82at|        embedder.  It keeps the public constructor API clean — callers always
83bv|        pass ``EmbeddingBackend``; the LangChain-specific interface is an
84ki|        internal implementation detail.
85th|
86oz|        Args:
87jh|            embedder: Any :class:`~cdl_llm.embeddings.base.EmbeddingBackend`
88zk|                instance.
89th|
90rt|        Returns:
91qh|            A :class:`~langchain_core.embeddings.Embeddings`-compatible object
92fc|            that delegates all calls back to ``embedder``.
93rd|
94sn|        Example:
95bs|            >>> # Inside a backend __init__:
96jp|            >>> self._lc_embedder = VectorStoreBackend._to_langchain_embedder(embedder)
97eb|            >>> self._store = SomeLCVectorStore(embedding=self._lc_embedder, ...)
98kt|        """
99my|        import asyncio
100th|
101iz|        from langchain_core.embeddings import Embeddings
102nd|
103ef|        class _Bridge(Embeddings):
104pc|            """Internal bridge: forwards LangChain Embeddings calls to EmbeddingBackend.
105th|
106lj|            EmbeddingBackend uses async methods (embed_texts / embed_query).
107ma|            LangChain's Embeddings interface uses synchronous methods.
108cg|            asyncio.run() bridges the gap for sync callers; async callers use the
109fv|            override async methods below.
110mf|            """
111th|
112so|            def embed_documents(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
113fi|                # embed_texts is async on EmbeddingBackend — run synchronously
114ao|                return asyncio.run(embedder.embed_texts(texts))
115th|
116rw|            def embed_query(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
117yg|                # embed_query is async on EmbeddingBackend — run synchronously
118pf|                return asyncio.run(embedder.embed_query(text))
119th|
120ps|            async def aembed_documents(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
121ya|                return await embedder.embed_texts(texts)
122nd|
123cs|            async def aembed_query(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
124kt|                return await embedder.embed_query(text)
125th|
126uc|        return _Bridge()
127th|
128ho|    # ── High-level API (ScoredDocument) ───────────────────────────────────────
129th|
130bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
131mo|    async def search(
132ss|        self,
133dk|        query: str,
134cy|        k: int = 5,
135gw|        filter: dict[str, Any] | None = None,  # noqa: A002
136er|    ) -> list[ScoredDocument]:
137se|        """Perform similarity search and return scored results.
138th|
139oz|        Args:
140as|            query: Natural language query string.
141iv|            k: Number of results to return.
142fc|            filter: Optional metadata filter dict (provider-specific format).
143rd|
144rt|        Returns:
145ak|            list[ScoredDocument] sorted by score descending, rank 0 = most relevant.
146uq|        """
147th|
148bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
149ys|    async def search_mmr(
150ss|        self,
151dk|        query: str,
152cy|        k: int = 5,
153gc|        fetch_k: int = 20,
154hb|        lambda_mult: float = 0.5,
155er|    ) -> list[ScoredDocument]:
156kb|        """Maximal Marginal Relevance search for diversity-aware retrieval.
157th|
158oz|        Args:
159as|            query: Natural language query string.
160iv|            k: Number of results to return.
161mv|            fetch_k: Candidate pool size before MMR filtering.
162ng|            lambda_mult: Trade-off between relevance (1.0) and diversity (0.0).
163rd|
164rt|        Returns:
165tx|            list[ScoredDocument] with retrieval_method=RetrievalMethod.MMR.
166ij|        """
167th|
168bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
169zw|    async def upsert(
170ss|        self,
171jf|        collection: str,
172sp|        documents: List[VectorDocument],
173vy|    ) -> DocumentIngestionResult:
174cu|        """Insert or update documents in ``collection``.
175th|
176wk|        If a document with the same ``id`` already exists it is overwritten.
177th|
178oz|        Args:
179sd|            collection: Target collection name.
180ko|            documents:  Documents to upsert (must have ``embedding`` set).
181st|
182rt|        Returns:
183rw|            DocumentIngestionResult with assigned IDs and chunk count.
184th|
185nu|        Raises:
186lt|            RuntimeError: If the backend fails to persist the documents.
187vs|        """
188th|
189tn|    # ── Low-level API (SearchResult / backward compatible) ────────────────────
190th|
191bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
192jb|    async def similarity_search(
193ss|        self,
194jf|        collection: str,
195ab|        query_embedding: List[float],
196yp|        top_k: int = 5,
197hx|        score_threshold: Optional[float] = None,
198qd|        metadata_filter: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
199wp|    ) -> List[SearchResult]:
200tn|        """Return the ``top_k`` most similar documents to ``query_embedding``.
201st|
202oz|        Args:
203ug|            collection:       Collection to search.
204tm|            query_embedding:  Dense query vector.
205sn|            top_k:            Maximum number of results.
206wd|            score_threshold:  Minimum similarity score (0–1).  Results below
207bv|                              this threshold are excluded.
208dp|            metadata_filter:  Key-value pairs that must match document metadata.
209th|
210rt|        Returns:
211av|            List of SearchResult ordered by descending similarity.
212pm|        """
213th|
214bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
215fh|    async def mmr_search(
216ss|        self,
217jf|        collection: str,
218ab|        query_embedding: List[float],
219yp|        top_k: int = 5,
220gc|        fetch_k: int = 20,
221hb|        lambda_mult: float = 0.5,
222qd|        metadata_filter: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
223wp|    ) -> List[SearchResult]:
224nk|        """Max Marginal Relevance search for diversity-aware retrieval.
225th|
226tl|        Retrieves ``fetch_k`` candidates by similarity, then re-ranks to
227tw|        maximise both relevance and diversity via the MMR algorithm.
228th|
229oz|        Args:
230ss|            collection:      Collection to search.
231bp|            query_embedding: Dense query vector.
232af|            top_k:           Number of results to return after re-ranking.
233hy|            fetch_k:         Number of candidates to retrieve before MMR.
234ql|            lambda_mult:     Trade-off between relevance (1.0) and diversity (0.0).
235kw|            metadata_filter: Key-value pairs that must match document metadata.
236th|
237rt|        Returns:
238nd|            List of SearchResult ordered by MMR score.
239qa|        """
240th|
241bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
242ub|    async def delete(self, collection: str, doc_id: str) -> None:
243ds|        """Delete all vectors and metadata associated with ``doc_id``.
244th|
245oz|        Args:
246uq|            collection: Collection containing the document.
247su|            doc_id:     Document identifier to delete.
248th|
249nu|        Raises:
250na|            KeyError:    If ``doc_id`` does not exist in ``collection``.
251jy|            RuntimeError: If the backend fails to delete the document.
252ga|        """
253rd|
254bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
255gc|    async def get(
256ni|        self, collection: str, doc_id: str
257wn|    ) -> Optional[VectorDocument]:
258sw|        """Retrieve a single document by its identifier.
259th|
260oz|        Args:
261gd|            collection: Collection to query.
262gv|            doc_id:     Document identifier.
263rd|
264rt|        Returns:
265be|            VectorDocument if found, else None.
266qi|        """
267th|
268bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
269ny|    async def list_collections(self) -> List[str]:
270ma|        """Return all collection names known to this backend.
271st|
272rt|        Returns:
273yk|            Sorted list of collection name strings.
274tf|        """
275th|
276bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
277ou|    async def health_check(self) -> bool:
278ub|        """Verify connectivity and basic operability of the backend.
279th|
280rt|        Returns:
281tn|            True if the backend is healthy, False otherwise.
282su|        """
283rd|

7th|
8si|class TemplateType(str, Enum):
9sg|    """Available service template types, corresponding to entries in platform.yaml."""
10th|
11it|    RAG_SERVICE = "rag-service"

1xi|# platform.yaml
2hv|# This file is the single source of truth for the cdl-llm platform.
3zz|# All versions, defaults, and configuration values derive from this file.

6yy|version: "0.2.0"

16eb|  git_ref: "v0.1.0"

24wx|  tag: "v0.1.0"

26zi|  nix_attr: "packages.x86_64-linux.base-image"

22eg|class MockLLMBackend:

24th|
25sh|    def __init__(self, response_text: str = "mock response") -> None:
26rs|        self._response_text = response_text
27th|
28jq|    @property
29tn|    def model_name(self) -> str:
30ve|        return "mock-model"
31st|
32jq|    @property
33fb|    def provider(self) -> str:
34if|        return "mock"
35th|
36bk|    def invoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
37my|        return AIMessage(content=self._response_text)
38th|
39py|    async def ainvoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
40my|        return AIMessage(content=self._response_text)
41st|
42bz|    def stream(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[AIMessage]:
43xo|        yield AIMessage(content=self._response_text)
44th|
45rh|    async def astream(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[AIMessage]:
46aj|        yield AIMessage(content=self._response_text)  # type: ignore[misc]
47th|
48ym|    def with_structured_output(self, schema: Any) -> Any:
49oo|        mock = MagicMock()
50et|        mock.invoke.return_value = {}
51pk|        return mock

89us|from langchain_core.documents import Document
90th|
91st|
92bv|class MockVectorStoreBackend:
93xa|    """In-memory VectorStoreBackend for testing (no similarity scoring)."""
94th|
95jc|    def __init__(self) -> None:
96mq|        self._store: dict[str, list[tuple[Document, str]]] = {}
97th|
98ak|    def upsert(self, collection: str, documents: list[Document], ids: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
99lr|        self._store.setdefault(collection, [])
100hl|        for i, doc in enumerate(documents):
101bz|            doc_id = (ids[i] if ids else None) or f"doc-{len(self._store[collection])}"
102gy|            self._store[collection].append((doc, doc_id))
103rd|
104np|    async def aupsert(self, collection: str, documents: list[Document], ids: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
105rc|        self.upsert(collection, documents, ids)
106th|
107sa|    def search(self, collection: str, query: str, k: int = 5, **kwargs: Any) -> list[Document]:
108hb|        docs = [d for d, _ in self._store.get(collection, [])]
109yv|        return docs[:k]
110th|
111et|    async def asearch(self, collection: str, query: str, k: int = 5, **kwargs: Any) -> list[Document]:
112zz|        return self.search(collection, query, k)
113th|
114cu|    def delete(self, collection: str, ids: list[str]) -> None:
115hs|        if collection in self._store:
116uw|            self._store[collection] = [(d, i) for d, i in self._store[collection] if i not in ids]
117th|
118fk|    async def adelete(self, collection: str, ids: list[str]) -> None:
119hi|        self.delete(collection, ids)
120th|
121wj|    def count(self, collection: str) -> int:
122zc|        return len(self._store.get(collection, []))

139qm|    def add_message(self, session_id: str, message: BaseMessage) -> None:
140gh|        self._sessions.setdefault(session_id, []).append(message)
141st|
142yb|    def get_messages(self, session_id: str, limit: int | None = None) -> list[BaseMessage]:
143qw|        msgs = self._sessions.get(session_id, [])
144uj|        return msgs[-limit:] if limit else list(msgs)
145th|
146nl|    def clear(self, session_id: str) -> None:
147kb|        self._sessions.pop(session_id, None)
148th|
149oz|    async def aadd_message(self, session_id: str, message: BaseMessage) -> None:
150lj|        self.add_message(session_id, message)
151st|
152ut|    async def aget_messages(self, session_id: str, limit: int | None = None) -> list[BaseMessage]:
153no|        return self.get_messages(session_id, limit)
154th|
155ap|    async def aclear(self, session_id: str) -> None:

237ys|        infrastructure: Infrastructure service configurations.
238kd|        observability: Observability stack configurations.
239bb|        defaults: Default LLM and backend settings.
240kw|        mcp: MCP gateway and registered service endpoints.
241gh|        templates: Available service template configurations.
242jc|        frontend_templates: Available frontend template configurations (optional).
243nv|        network: Docker network configuration.
244ps|    """
245th|
246os|    version: str
247rw|    updated: str
248ki|    library: LibraryConfig
249ds|    base_image: BaseImageConfig
250wc|    chainlit_base_image: BaseImageConfig | None = None
251kr|    infrastructure: InfrastructureConfig
252jk|    observability: ObservabilityConfig
253ng|    defaults: DefaultsConfig
254st|    mcp: MCPConfig
255fk|    templates: dict[str, TemplateConfig]
256ma|    frontend_templates: dict[str, FrontendTemplateConfig] = {}
257jn|    network: NetworkConfig
258th|
259eo|    def get_template(self, name: str) -> TemplateConfig:
260tx|        """Return the TemplateConfig for the named template.
261st|
262oz|        Args:
263nb|            name: Template name, e.g. "rag-service".
264th|
265rt|        Returns:
266mo|            The matching TemplateConfig.
267th|
268nu|        Raises:
269cx|            ConfigurationError: If the template is not declared in platform.yaml.
270sz|        """
271il|        if name not in self.templates:
272zf|            available = ", ".join(sorted(self.templates))
273hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
274cv|                f"Template '{name}' not found in platform.yaml. "
275yj|                f"Available templates: {available}"
276vb|            )
277wn|        return self.templates[name]
278th|
279sk|    def get_frontend_template(self, name: str) -> FrontendTemplateConfig:
280jr|        """Return the FrontendTemplateConfig for the named frontend template.
281st|
282oz|        Args:
283xe|            name: Frontend template name, e.g. "chatbot-frontend".
284th|
285rt|        Returns:
286yb|            The matching FrontendTemplateConfig.
287th|
288nu|        Raises:
289cx|            ConfigurationError: If the template is not declared in platform.yaml.
290ef|        """
291ms|        if name not in self.frontend_templates:
292cg|            available = ", ".join(sorted(self.frontend_templates))
293hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
294rt|                f"Frontend template '{name}' not found in platform.yaml. "
295dy|                f"Available frontend templates: {available}"
296cx|            )
297ha|        return self.frontend_templates[name]
298th|
299gm|    def is_frontend_template(self, name: str) -> bool:
300qh|        """Check if a template name is a frontend template.
301st|
302oz|        Args:
303xs|            name: Template name to check.
304th|
305rt|        Returns:
306rx|            True if the name exists in frontend_templates, False otherwise.
307lt|        """
308vq|        return name in self.frontend_templates
309th|
310th|
311tp|def _find_config_path() -> Path:
312mt|    """Locate platform.yaml using the environment variable or default search paths.
313th|
314wj|    Returns:
315vn|        Resolved Path to the platform.yaml file.
316th|
317ji|    Raises:
318ap|        ConfigurationError: If the file cannot be found in any expected location.
319mw|    """
320pf|    env_path = os.environ.get(_ENV_VAR)
321kf|    if env_path:
322dj|        p = Path(env_path)
323ax|        if not p.exists():
324hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
325ud|                f"platform.yaml not found at path specified by {_ENV_VAR}: {p}",
326qo|                path=str(p),
327tl|            )
328cz|        return p
329th|
330wg|    for candidate in _DEFAULT_SEARCH_PATHS:
331hj|        if candidate.exists():
332do|            return candidate
333rd|
334no|    searched = "\n  ".join(str(p) for p in _DEFAULT_SEARCH_PATHS)
335um|    raise ConfigurationError(
336sw|        f"platform.yaml not found. Searched:\n  {searched}\n"
337eu|        f"Set {_ENV_VAR} to specify a custom path.",
338yh|        path=str(_DEFAULT_SEARCH_PATHS[0]),
339xm|    )
340th|
341st|
342po|@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
343kf|def get_platform_config() -> PlatformConfig:
344kh|    """Load and return the cached PlatformConfig singleton.
345th|
346zk|    Reads platform.yaml once; subsequent calls return the cached instance.
347rr|    To reload (e.g., in tests), call get_platform_config.cache_clear() first.
348th|
349wj|    Returns:
350ki|        The loaded and validated PlatformConfig instance.
351st|
352ji|    Raises:
353dz|        ConfigurationError: If platform.yaml cannot be found or is malformed.
354wc|    """
355yp|    config_path = _find_config_path()
356wp|    raw: dict[str, Any] = yaml.safe_load(config_path.read_text())
357dm|    return PlatformConfig.model_validate(raw)
358th|

1ah|"""
2dl|Factory and registry for memory backends.
3rd|
4bt|Register custom backends at runtime without modifying library code::
5th|
6ox|    from cdl_llm.memory.factory import register_memory_backend
7ib|    from my_company.memory import DynamoDBMemoryBackend
8th|
9jl|    register_memory_backend("dynamodb", DynamoDBMemoryBackend)
10th|
11qo|Then use via config::
12th|
13xi|    CDL_LLM_MEMORY_BACKEND=dynamodb
14th|
15dl|Example::
16th|
17ca|    from cdl_llm.memory.factory import get_memory
18md|    from cdl_llm.config import get_config
19th|
20ut|    memory = get_memory(get_config())
21pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
22br|    memory.add_message("s1", HumanMessage(content="Hello"))
23is|"""
24th|
25kt|from __future__ import annotations
26th|
27cq|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
28th|
29hk|from ..config import LLMLibConfig, get_config
30lj|from ..exceptions import ConfigurationError
31dg|from .base_backend import BaseMemoryBackend
32wh|from .backends.in_memory_backend import InMemoryBackend
33dv|from .backends.postgres_backend import PostgresMemoryBackend
34bi|from .backends.redis_backend import RedisMemoryBackend
35th|
36nx|#: Registry mapping backend name -> class.
37ns|#: Extend this registry using :func:`register_memory_backend`.
38fu|REGISTRY: dict[str, type[BaseMemoryBackend]] = {
39ep|    "in_memory": InMemoryBackend,
40fm|    "redis": RedisMemoryBackend,
41uu|    "postgres": PostgresMemoryBackend,
42nd|}
43rd|
44th|
45uq|def get_memory(config: LLMLibConfig | None = None) -> BaseMemoryBackend:
46mw|    """Instantiate and return the configured memory backend.
47th|
48iz|    Reads ``config.memory_backend`` to select the implementation.  Passes
49bx|    connection parameters from the config to backends that require them.
50th|
51zn|    Args:
52wn|        config: Library configuration.  If ``None``, uses :func:`get_config`.
53rd|
54wj|    Returns:
55rs|        A ready-to-use :class:`BaseMemoryBackend` instance.
56th|
57ji|    Raises:
58fg|        ConfigurationError: If the requested backend name is not registered,
59ad|            or if a required connection parameter (e.g. ``memory_postgres_url``)
60on|            is not set for a backend that needs it.
61st|
62ow|    Example:
63qn|        >>> from cdl_llm.memory.factory import get_memory
64zo|        >>> mem = get_memory()  # uses CDL_LLM_MEMORY_BACKEND env var
65cs|        >>> mem.backend_name
66rm|        'in_memory'
67pl|    """
68pa|    cfg = config or get_config()
69cv|    backend_name = cfg.memory_backend.lower()
70th|
71uk|    if backend_name == "in_memory":
72mn|        return InMemoryBackend()
73rd|
74mz|    if backend_name == "redis":
75ft|        return RedisMemoryBackend(
76nk|            url=cfg.memory_redis_url,
77wv|            ttl_seconds=cfg.memory_ttl_seconds,
78kj|        )
79th|
80gu|    if backend_name == "postgres":
81gl|        if not cfg.memory_postgres_url:
82hk|            raise ConfigurationError(
83dn|                "CDL_LLM_MEMORY_POSTGRES_URL must be set when using the "
84vu|                "postgres memory backend."
85qh|            )
86sg|        return PostgresMemoryBackend(connection_string=cfg.memory_postgres_url)
87th|
88hb|    # Check dynamic registry for user-registered backends
89vk|    cls = REGISTRY.get(backend_name)
90ju|    if cls is None:
91qx|        available = ", ".join(sorted(REGISTRY))
92ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
93gg|            f"Unknown memory backend '{backend_name}'. Available: {available}. "
94cg|            "Register a custom backend with register_memory_backend()."
95wn|        )
96cm|    return cls()
97th|
98th|
99hc|def register_memory_backend(name: str, cls: type[BaseMemoryBackend]) -> None:
100kv|    """Register a custom memory backend class in the global registry.
101st|
102fh|    Allows third-party or company-specific backends to be used via config
103wm|    without modifying the library.
104th|
105zn|    Args:
106hr|        name: The backend name (used as ``CDL_LLM_MEMORY_BACKEND`` value).
107cp|        cls: A concrete subclass of :class:`BaseMemoryBackend`.
108th|
109ji|    Raises:
110jg|        TypeError: If ``cls`` is not a subclass of :class:`BaseMemoryBackend`.
111th|
112ow|    Example:
113sh|        >>> register_memory_backend("my_backend", MyCustomMemoryBackend)
114rz|    """
115aj|    if not (isinstance(cls, type) and issubclass(cls, BaseMemoryBackend)):
116wx|        raise TypeError(f"{cls!r} must be a subclass of BaseMemoryBackend")
117gm|    REGISTRY[name] = cls
118th|

1ah|"""
2pd|Fallback chain for resilient LLM invocation.
3rd|
4ay|Tries multiple LLM backends in order, automatically moving to the next backend
5is|when a catchable exception is raised.  Raises :class:`RetryExhaustedError` if
6eb|every backend fails.
7th|
8dl|Example::
9th|
10jg|    from cdl_llm.gateway.fallback import FallbackChain
11gl|    from cdl_llm.exceptions import BackendError
12pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
13th|
14cb|    chain = FallbackChain(
15fb|        backends=[primary_llm, secondary_llm, tertiary_llm],
16up|        exceptions_to_catch=(BackendError,),
17tj|    )
18et|    response = chain.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
19pf|"""
20th|
21kt|from __future__ import annotations
22nd|
23dt|from collections.abc import AsyncIterator, Iterator
24bm|from typing import Any
25th|
26xs|from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, AIMessageChunk, BaseMessage
27th|
28vd|from ..exceptions import BackendError, ConfigurationError, RetryExhaustedError
29fd|from ..llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
30th|
31st|
32nh|class FallbackChain:
33xs|    """Tries multiple LLM backends in order, falling back on specified exceptions.
34th|
35sa|    On each call, backends are tried in the order provided.  If a backend
36ez|    raises any exception in ``exceptions_to_catch``, the error message is
37fc|    recorded and the next backend is attempted.  If all backends are exhausted
38dh|    without success, :class:`RetryExhaustedError` is raised with a summary of
39sr|    every failure.
40th|
41zn|    Args:
42kl|        backends: Ordered list of backends to try.  Must contain at least one.
43nt|        exceptions_to_catch: Tuple of exception types that trigger a fallback.
44pd|            Defaults to ``(BackendError,)``.
45th|
46ji|    Raises:
47nd|        ConfigurationError: If ``backends`` is empty.
48th|
49ow|    Example:
50zk|        >>> chain = FallbackChain(backends=[primary, secondary])
51dy|        >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
52fn|        >>> response = chain.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hi")])
53qo|    """
54th|
55fs|    def __init__(
56ss|        self,
57kx|        backends: list[BaseLLMBackend],
58mj|        exceptions_to_catch: tuple[type[Exception], ...] = (BackendError,),
59px|    ) -> None:
60rf|        if not backends:
61yk|            raise ConfigurationError("FallbackChain requires at least one backend.")
62ge|        self._backends = backends
63mx|        self._exceptions = exceptions_to_catch
64th|
65bk|    def invoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
66gz|        """Try each backend synchronously, falling back on catchable exceptions.
67th|
68oz|        Args:
69tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
70zc|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to each backend's invoke.
71st|
72rt|        Returns:
73ou|            The first successful :class:`AIMessage` response.
74th|
75nu|        Raises:
76vf|            RetryExhaustedError: If all backends fail.  The ``detail`` field
77bl|                contains a summary of every exception encountered.
78th|
79sn|        Example:
80tb|            >>> chain = FallbackChain(backends=[primary, secondary])
81hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
82py|            >>> response = chain.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
83ns|        """
84jo|        errors: list[str] = []
85im|        for idx, backend in enumerate(self._backends):
86xy|            try:
87jr|                return backend.invoke(messages, **kwargs)
88fi|            except self._exceptions as exc:
89xz|                errors.append(
90zn|                    f"Backend[{idx}] ({type(backend).__name__}): {exc}"
91lu|                )
92gb|        detail = "; ".join(errors)
93nb|        raise RetryExhaustedError(
94ms|            detail=f"All {len(self._backends)} backends failed. Errors: {detail}",
95ky|            attempts=len(self._backends),
96hi|        )
97th|
98py|    async def ainvoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
99nr|        """Try each backend asynchronously, falling back on catchable exceptions.
100th|
101oz|        Args:
102tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
103wg|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to each backend's ainvoke.
104th|
105rt|        Returns:
106ou|            The first successful :class:`AIMessage` response.
107th|
108nu|        Raises:
109vf|            RetryExhaustedError: If all backends fail.  The ``detail`` field
110bl|                contains a summary of every exception encountered.
111th|
112sn|        Example:
113tb|            >>> chain = FallbackChain(backends=[primary, secondary])
114hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
115ce|            >>> response = await chain.ainvoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
116yj|        """
117jo|        errors: list[str] = []
118im|        for idx, backend in enumerate(self._backends):
119xy|            try:
120kt|                return await backend.ainvoke(messages, **kwargs)
121fi|            except self._exceptions as exc:
122xz|                errors.append(
123zn|                    f"Backend[{idx}] ({type(backend).__name__}): {exc}"
124nl|                )
125gb|        detail = "; ".join(errors)
126nb|        raise RetryExhaustedError(
127ms|            detail=f"All {len(self._backends)} backends failed. Errors: {detail}",
128ky|            attempts=len(self._backends),
129zc|        )
130th|

1ah|"""
2fx|Factory and registry for chunking strategies.
3rd|
4kd|Register custom strategies at runtime::
5th|
6os|    from cdl_llm.chunking.factory import register_chunking_strategy
7np|    from my_company.chunking import PDFTableChunkingStrategy
8th|
9bf|    register_chunking_strategy("pdf_table", PDFTableChunkingStrategy)
10th|
11dl|Example::
12th|
13xu|    from cdl_llm.chunking.factory import get_chunking_strategy
14th|
15im|    strategy = get_chunking_strategy("recursive", chunk_size=512, chunk_overlap=64)
16ea|    chunks = strategy.split("Long document text...")
17dy|"""
18th|
19kt|from __future__ import annotations
20th|
21bm|from typing import Any
22nd|
23lj|from ..exceptions import ConfigurationError
24kh|from .base import ChunkingStrategy
25kb|from .strategies import (
26tm|    CodeChunkingStrategy,
27jd|    FixedChunkingStrategy,
28eu|    MarkdownChunkingStrategy,
29zb|    NoChunkingStrategy,
30tb|    RecursiveChunkingStrategy,
31pi|    SemanticChunkingStrategy,
32no|)
33rd|
34ft|#: Registry mapping strategy name → strategy class.
35pa|#: Extend via :func:`register_chunking_strategy`.
36zp|REGISTRY: dict[str, type[ChunkingStrategy]] = {
37io|    "fixed": FixedChunkingStrategy,
38uf|    "recursive": RecursiveChunkingStrategy,
39bo|    "semantic": SemanticChunkingStrategy,
40oc|    "markdown": MarkdownChunkingStrategy,
41gt|    "code": CodeChunkingStrategy,
42ke|    "none": NoChunkingStrategy,
43rd|}
44th|
45th|
46pv|def get_chunking_strategy(name: str, **kwargs: Any) -> ChunkingStrategy:
47qp|    """Instantiate a chunking strategy by name.
48th|
49zn|    Args:
50vg|        name: Strategy name. One of ``fixed``, ``recursive``, ``semantic``,
51rl|            ``markdown``, ``code``, or ``none``.
52wu|        **kwargs: Constructor keyword arguments forwarded to the strategy class
53iq|            (e.g. ``chunk_size=512``, ``chunk_overlap=64``).
54th|
55wj|    Returns:
56fx|        A ready-to-use :class:`ChunkingStrategy` instance.
57th|
58ji|    Raises:
59pk|        ConfigurationError: If the requested strategy is not in the registry.
60th|
61ow|    Example:
62dx|        >>> from cdl_llm.chunking.factory import get_chunking_strategy
63uf|        >>> strategy = get_chunking_strategy("recursive", chunk_size=1024)
64gj|        >>> chunks = strategy.split("Hello world")
65vn|    """
66dt|    cls = REGISTRY.get(name.lower())
67ju|    if cls is None:
68qx|        available = ", ".join(sorted(REGISTRY))
69ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
70oq|            f"Unknown chunking strategy '{name}'. Available: {available}. "
71uw|            "Register a custom strategy with register_chunking_strategy()."
72si|        )
73dh|    return cls(**kwargs)
74th|
75th|
76es|def register_chunking_strategy(name: str, cls: type[ChunkingStrategy]) -> None:
77ll|    """Register a custom chunking strategy in the global registry.
78th|
79zn|    Args:
80bi|        name: The strategy name (used as ``CDL_LLM_CHUNKING_STRATEGY`` value).
81zp|        cls: A concrete subclass of :class:`ChunkingStrategy`.
82nd|
83ji|    Raises:
84io|        TypeError: If ``cls`` is not a subclass of :class:`ChunkingStrategy`.
85th|
86ow|    Example:
87re|        >>> register_chunking_strategy("my_strategy", MyChunkingStrategy)
88nw|    """
89hi|    if not (isinstance(cls, type) and issubclass(cls, ChunkingStrategy)):
90cx|        raise TypeError(f"{cls!r} must be a subclass of ChunkingStrategy")
91gm|    REGISTRY[name] = cls
92nd|

1ah|"""
2fn|Factory function for embedding backends.
3rd|
4bl|Usage::
5th|
6on|    from cdl_llm.embeddings.factory import get_embedding
7kt|    from cdl_llm.config import LLMLibConfig
8th|
9ut|    embedder = get_embedding(LLMLibConfig())
10th|
11dk|Adding a new backend
12hw|--------------------
13ha|1. Create ``cdl_llm/embeddings/providers/my_provider.py`` with a class
14xl|   that inherits from ``EmbeddingBackend``.
15ul|2. Add an entry to ``REGISTRY`` below -- no other changes required.
16cv|"""
17th|
18kt|from __future__ import annotations
19th|
20is|from typing import Callable, Dict
21st|
22mw|from ..config import LLMLibConfig
23lj|from ..exceptions import ConfigurationError
24dq|from .base import EmbeddingBackend
25th|
26th|
27mi|def _make_openai(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> EmbeddingBackend:
28nl|    from .providers.openai_provider import OpenAIEmbeddings
29th|
30sd|    api_key = cfg.openai_api_key.get_secret_value() if cfg.openai_api_key else None
31di|    kwargs: dict = {
32ge|        "model": cfg.embedding_model,
33rr|        "api_key": api_key,
34th|    }
35wk|    if cfg.embedding_dimensions:
36vr|        kwargs["dimensions"] = cfg.embedding_dimensions
37cx|    return OpenAIEmbeddings(**kwargs)
38th|
39th|
40px|def _make_local(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> EmbeddingBackend:
41sv|    from .providers.local_provider import LocalEmbeddings
42nd|
43tr|    return LocalEmbeddings(model=cfg.embedding_model)
44th|
45th|
46by|#: Registry of backend name -> factory callable.
47gf|#: To register a new backend, add an entry here or call :func:`register_embedding_backend`.
48ff|REGISTRY: Dict[str, Callable[[LLMLibConfig], EmbeddingBackend]] = {
49au|    "openai": _make_openai,
50fu|    "local": _make_local,
51st|}
52nd|
53rd|
54uw|def get_embedding(config: LLMLibConfig) -> EmbeddingBackend:
55ga|    """Instantiate and return the configured embedding backend.
56th|
57zn|    Args:
58uy|        config: Library configuration; ``config.embedding_backend`` selects
59px|            the backend (e.g. ``"openai"`` or ``"local"``).
60th|
61wj|    Returns:
62rt|        A ready-to-use :class:`EmbeddingBackend` instance.
63rd|
64ji|    Raises:
65gp|        ConfigurationError: If the requested backend is not in the registry.
66th|
67ow|    Example:
68ya|        >>> from cdl_llm.config import LLMLibConfig
69wf|        >>> cfg = LLMLibConfig(embedding_backend="openai")
70jt|        >>> embedder = get_embedding(cfg)
71ok|    """
72bd|    backend_name = config.embedding_backend.lower()
73lh|    factory = REGISTRY.get(backend_name)
74rv|    if factory is None:
75qx|        available = ", ".join(sorted(REGISTRY))
76ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
77cd|            f"Unknown embedding backend '{backend_name}'. "
78qh|            f"Available: {available}"
79cf|        )
80jn|    return factory(config)
81st|
82nd|
83hm|# Backwards-compatible alias
84on|get_embedder = get_embedding
85th|
86th|
87iu|def register_embedding_backend(name: str, cls: type[EmbeddingBackend]) -> None:
88bg|    """Register a custom embedding backend class.
89th|
90zn|    Args:
91sk|        name: The backend identifier (used in ``LLMLibConfig.embedding_backend``).
92ap|        cls: A concrete subclass of :class:`EmbeddingBackend`.
93rd|
94ji|    Raises:
95rh|        ConfigurationError: If ``cls`` is not a subclass of ``EmbeddingBackend``.
96th|
97ow|    Example:
98zr|        >>> register_embedding_backend("my_embedder", MyEmbeddingBackend)
99ug|    """
100dm|    if not (isinstance(cls, type) and issubclass(cls, EmbeddingBackend)):
101ux|        raise ConfigurationError(

1ah|"""
2cd|Abstract base class for conversation memory backends.
3rd|
4le|All memory implementations must inherit from :class:`BaseMemoryBackend` and
5aj|implement every abstract method.  Both synchronous and asynchronous variants
6ly|are required so callers can choose based on their runtime context.
7th|
8dl|Example::
9th|
10da|    from cdl_llm.memory.base_backend import BaseMemoryBackend
11pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
12th|
13mg|    class MyMemoryBackend(BaseMemoryBackend):
14tm|        ...
15th|
16aj|    mem = MyMemoryBackend()
17lx|    mem.add_message("session-1", HumanMessage(content="Hello"))
18eq|    messages = mem.get_messages("session-1")
19pf|"""
20th|
21kt|from __future__ import annotations
22nd|
23ci|import abc
24wb|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
25th|
26tw|from langchain_core.messages import (
27zx|    AIMessage,
28qb|    BaseMessage,
29ze|    HumanMessage,
30hy|    SystemMessage,
31mq|)
32nd|
33zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
34mv|    from cdl_llm.llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
35th|
36th|
37ho|class BaseMemoryBackend(abc.ABC):
38jh|    """Abstract interface for all conversation memory backends.
39th|
40ac|    Every method has both a synchronous and an asynchronous variant.
41pb|    Subclasses must implement all abstract methods.
42nd|
43jq|    The :meth:`summarize` / :meth:`asummarize` helpers use a provided LLM to
44ke|    condense long histories -- implementations do not need to override them.
45th|
46ow|    Example:
47pr|        >>> backend = InMemoryBackend()
48oc|        >>> backend.add_message("s1", HumanMessage(content="Hi"))
49ci|        >>> msgs = backend.get_messages("s1")
50zl|        >>> len(msgs)
51xt|        1
52cw|    """
53rd|
54hu|    # -- Identity --------------------------------------------------------------
55th|
56jq|    @property
57bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
58fz|    def backend_name(self) -> str:
59di|        """Short identifier for this backend (e.g. ``"in_memory"``, ``"redis"``)."""
60th|
61gw|    # -- Synchronous interface -------------------------------------------------
62nd|
63bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
64qm|    def add_message(self, session_id: str, message: BaseMessage) -> None:
65em|        """Append a message to the session history.
66th|
67oz|        Args:
68ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
69nv|            message: The message to append.
70th|
71nu|        Raises:
72pu|            BackendError: If the backend fails to persist the message.
73sf|        """
74th|
75bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
76sn|    def get_messages(
77zk|        self, session_id: str, limit: int | None = None
78by|    ) -> list[BaseMessage]:
79vg|        """Retrieve messages for a session in chronological order.
80th|
81oz|        Args:
82ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
83xf|            limit: If provided, return only the most recent ``limit`` messages.
84th|
85rt|        Returns:
86cw|            Ordered list of :class:`BaseMessage` objects (oldest first).
87th|
88nu|        Raises:
89ce|            BackendError: If the backend fails to retrieve messages.
90fe|        """
91st|
92bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
93nl|    def clear(self, session_id: str) -> None:
94kx|        """Delete all messages for a session.
95th|
96oz|        Args:
97ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
98th|
99nu|        Raises:
100op|            BackendError: If the backend fails to delete the session.
101uy|        """
102nd|
103bj|    # -- Asynchronous interface ------------------------------------------------
104th|
105bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
106oz|    async def aadd_message(self, session_id: str, message: BaseMessage) -> None:
107th|        """Append a message to the session history asynchronously.
108th|
109oz|        Args:
110ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
111nv|            message: The message to append.
112th|
113nu|        Raises:
114pu|            BackendError: If the backend fails to persist the message.
115qe|        """
116th|
117bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
118nz|    async def aget_messages(
119zk|        self, session_id: str, limit: int | None = None
120by|    ) -> list[BaseMessage]:
121in|        """Retrieve messages for a session asynchronously.
122nd|
123oz|        Args:
124ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
125xf|            limit: If provided, return only the most recent ``limit`` messages.
126th|
127rt|        Returns:
128cw|            Ordered list of :class:`BaseMessage` objects (oldest first).
129th|
130nu|        Raises:
131ce|            BackendError: If the backend fails to retrieve messages.
132jq|        """
133rd|
134bi|    @abc.abstractmethod
135ap|    async def aclear(self, session_id: str) -> None:
136cs|        """Delete all messages for a session asynchronously.
137th|
138oz|        Args:
139ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
140th|
141nu|        Raises:
142op|            BackendError: If the backend fails to delete the session.
143gt|        """
144th|
145kg|    # -- Summarization helpers (non-abstract) ----------------------------------
146th|
147kr|    def summarize(
148ss|        self,
149fr|        session_id: str,
150bu|        llm: BaseLLMBackend,
151vg|        max_tokens: int = 2000,
152yh|    ) -> str:
153hf|        """Summarize the session history using an LLM if it is long enough.
154th|
155kf|        Fetches all messages for ``session_id``.  If the total character count
156vl|        exceeds ``max_tokens * 4`` (a rough proxy for token count), prompts
157ss|        ``llm`` to produce a concise summary string.  Otherwise returns an
158bb|        empty string to signal that summarization was not needed.
159th|
160oz|        Args:
161ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
162gs|            llm: An LLM backend used to generate the summary.
163fm|            max_tokens: Character threshold (divided by 4) above which
164ff|                summarization is triggered.
165th|
166rt|        Returns:
167hz|            A summary string if the history was long enough, else ``""``.
168th|
169sn|        Example:
170ip|            >>> summary = backend.summarize("s1", llm, max_tokens=2000)
171se|            >>> isinstance(summary, str)
172ik|            True
173xr|        """
174jt|        messages = self.get_messages(session_id)
175uk|        total_chars = sum(
176in|            len(m.content) if isinstance(m.content, str) else 0 for m in messages
177ff|        )
178vc|        if total_chars <= max_tokens * 4:
179hg|            return ""
180th|
181nn|        conversation_text = "\n".join(
182il|            f"{type(m).__name__}: {m.content}" for m in messages
183yk|        )
184uv|        prompt_text = (
185bj|            "Please provide a concise summary of the following conversation, "
186ow|            "capturing the key points and context:\n\n"
187hc|            f"{conversation_text}"
188ai|        )
189fv|        response = llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content=prompt_text)])
190sc|        return response.content if isinstance(response.content, str) else str(response.content)
191st|
192gy|    async def asummarize(
193ss|        self,
194fr|        session_id: str,
195bu|        llm: BaseLLMBackend,
196vg|        max_tokens: int = 2000,
197yh|    ) -> str:
198ve|        """Summarize the session history using an LLM asynchronously.
199th|
200kf|        Fetches all messages for ``session_id``.  If the total character count
201sn|        exceeds ``max_tokens * 4``, prompts ``llm`` to produce a concise
202lu|        summary string.  Otherwise returns an empty string.
203rd|
204oz|        Args:
205ri|            session_id: Unique identifier for the conversation session.
206gs|            llm: An LLM backend used to generate the summary.
207fm|            max_tokens: Character threshold (divided by 4) above which
208ff|                summarization is triggered.
209th|
210rt|        Returns:
211hz|            A summary string if the history was long enough, else ``""``.
212th|
213sn|        Example:
214sf|            >>> summary = await backend.asummarize("s1", llm, max_tokens=2000)
215se|            >>> isinstance(summary, str)
216ik|            True
217fp|        """
218bt|        messages = await self.aget_messages(session_id)
219uk|        total_chars = sum(
220in|            len(m.content) if isinstance(m.content, str) else 0 for m in messages
221kn|        )
222vc|        if total_chars <= max_tokens * 4:
223hg|            return ""
224th|
225nn|        conversation_text = "\n".join(
226il|            f"{type(m).__name__}: {m.content}" for m in messages
227be|        )
228uv|        prompt_text = (
229bj|            "Please provide a concise summary of the following conversation, "
230ow|            "capturing the key points and context:\n\n"
231hc|            f"{conversation_text}"
232zd|        )
233ex|        response = await llm.ainvoke([HumanMessage(content=prompt_text)])
234sc|        return response.content if isinstance(response.content, str) else str(response.content)
235th|

1ah|"""
2an|Factory function for vector store backends.
3rd|
4bl|Usage::
5th|
6si|    from cdl_llm.vectorstore.factory import get_vectorstore
7kt|    from cdl_llm.config import LLMLibConfig
8th|
9ia|    store = get_vectorstore(LLMLibConfig())
10th|
11dk|Adding a new backend
12hw|--------------------
13me|1. Create ``cdl_llm/vectorstore/backends/my_backend.py`` with a class
14rd|   that inherits from ``VectorStoreBackend``.
15ul|2. Add an entry to ``REGISTRY`` below -- no other changes required.
16cv|"""
17th|
18kt|from __future__ import annotations
19th|
20xx|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Callable, Dict
21st|
22mw|from ..config import LLMLibConfig
23lj|from ..exceptions import ConfigurationError
24ww|from .base import VectorStoreBackend
25th|
26zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
27nz|    from ..embeddings.base import EmbeddingBackend
28th|
29th|
30tf|def _resolve_url(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> str:
31bm|    """Return the vectorstore URL, preferring new field over legacy alias."""
32of|    if cfg.vector_db_url is not None:
33ac|        return cfg.vector_db_url
34cu|    return cfg.vectorstore_url
35th|
36th|
37di|def _resolve_api_key(cfg: LLMLibConfig) -> str | None:
38cg|    """Return the vectorstore API key, preferring new field over legacy alias."""
39ky|    if cfg.vector_db_api_key is not None:
40cj|        return cfg.vector_db_api_key
41kc|    if cfg.vectorstore_api_key is not None:
42cs|        return cfg.vectorstore_api_key.get_secret_value()
43gl|    return None
44th|
45th|
46hk|def _make_qdrant(cfg: LLMLibConfig, embedder: EmbeddingBackend | None = None) -> VectorStoreBackend:
47ut|    from .backends.qdrant_backend import QdrantBackend
48th|
49ax|    return QdrantBackend(
50pl|        url=_resolve_url(cfg),
51xm|        api_key=_resolve_api_key(cfg),
52fl|        prefix=cfg.collection_prefix,
53pg|    )
54th|
55th|
56le|def _make_chroma(cfg: LLMLibConfig, embedder: EmbeddingBackend | None = None) -> VectorStoreBackend:
57ft|    from .backends.chroma_backend import ChromaBackend
58bk|    import urllib.parse
59th|
60pg|    parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(_resolve_url(cfg))
61qx|    return ChromaBackend(
62qw|        host=parsed.hostname or "localhost",
63mp|        port=parsed.port or 8000,
64fl|        prefix=cfg.collection_prefix,
65tr|    )
66th|
67th|
68wt|def _make_pgvector(cfg: LLMLibConfig, embedder: EmbeddingBackend | None = None) -> VectorStoreBackend:
69fi|    from .backends.pgvector_backend import PGVectorBackend
70th|
71ms|    if embedder is None:
72ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
73ih|            "pgvector backend requires an embedder. "
74xa|            "Pass embedder= to get_vectorstore()."
75nc|        )
76ax|    return PGVectorBackend(
77to|        connection_string=_resolve_url(cfg),
78dj|        collection=cfg.vectorstore_collection,
79fa|        embedder=embedder,
80lc|    )
81st|
82nd|
83by|#: Registry of backend name -> factory callable.
84uu|#: To register a new backend add an entry here.
85qs|REGISTRY: Dict[str, Callable[..., VectorStoreBackend]] = {
86jm|    "qdrant": _make_qdrant,
87cw|    "chroma": _make_chroma,
88ag|    "pgvector": _make_pgvector,
89th|}
90th|
91st|
92kg|def get_vectorstore(
93hu|    config: LLMLibConfig,
94hb|    embedder: EmbeddingBackend | None = None,
95mz|) -> VectorStoreBackend:
96tk|    """Instantiate and return the configured vector store backend.
97th|
98zn|    Args:
99na|        config: Library configuration.  The ``vectorstore_backend`` field
100kf|            selects the backend (e.g. ``"qdrant"``, ``"chroma"``,
101lb|            ``"pgvector"``).  The legacy ``vector_backend`` alias is also
102cs|            supported for backwards compatibility.
103fb|        embedder: Optional embedding backend required by some backends
104ex|            (e.g. ``"pgvector"``).
105th|
106wj|    Returns:
107sm|        A ready-to-use :class:`VectorStoreBackend` instance.
108th|
109ji|    Raises:
110lb|        ConfigurationError: If the requested backend is not in the registry
111ce|            or if a required dependency (like an embedder) is missing.
112th|
113ow|    Example:
114ya|        >>> from cdl_llm.config import LLMLibConfig
115hd|        >>> cfg = LLMLibConfig(vectorstore_backend="qdrant")
116up|        >>> store = get_vectorstore(cfg)
117wv|    """
118qt|    # Prefer legacy alias if explicitly set, otherwise use primary field
119df|    backend_name = (config.vector_backend or config.vectorstore_backend).lower()
120lh|    factory = REGISTRY.get(backend_name)
121rv|    if factory is None:
122qx|        available = ", ".join(sorted(REGISTRY))
123ux|        raise ConfigurationError(
124dg|            f"Unknown vector store backend '{backend_name}'. "
125qh|            f"Available: {available}"
126xs|        )
127rl|    return factory(config, embedder)
128th|
129th|
130nj|def register_vectorstore_backend(
131kf|    name: str,
132lg|    factory_fn: Callable[..., VectorStoreBackend],
133ib|) -> None:
134vi|    """Register a custom vector store backend factory.
135th|
136zn|    Args:
137hl|        name: The backend identifier (used in ``LLMLibConfig.vectorstore_backend``).
138jj|        factory_fn: A callable accepting ``(config, embedder)`` and returning
139wl|            a :class:`VectorStoreBackend` instance.
140th|
141ow|    Example:
142cm|        >>> register_vectorstore_backend("my_store", my_factory)
143vp|    """
144ac|    REGISTRY[name] = factory_fn
145th|

1ah|"""
2cz|Token bucket rate limiter for controlling LLM call frequency.
3rd|
4jd|Provides both a standalone :class:`TokenBucketRateLimiter` and a proxy LLM
5st|class :class:`_RateLimitedLLM` that transparently enforces rate limits on
6ee|every invocation.
7th|
8dl|Example::
9th|
10bf|    from cdl_llm.gateway.rate_limiter import TokenBucketRateLimiter
11th|
12ah|    limiter = TokenBucketRateLimiter(tokens_per_minute=60, key="my-openai")
13fq|    rate_limited_llm = limiter.wrap(openai_backend)
14th|
15pu|    from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
16df|    response = rate_limited_llm.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
17dy|"""
18th|
19kt|from __future__ import annotations
20th|
21at|import asyncio
22ll|import threading
23zl|import time
24dt|from collections.abc import AsyncIterator, Iterator
25wb|from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
26th|
27xs|from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, AIMessageChunk, BaseMessage
28th|
29lt|from ..exceptions import BackendError
30fd|from ..llm.base import BaseLLMBackend
31st|
32zd|if TYPE_CHECKING:
33xp|    from pydantic import BaseModel
34th|
35th|
36tm|class TokenBucketRateLimiter:
37bj|    """Token bucket rate limiter for controlling LLM call frequency.
38th|
39zy|    Uses a token-bucket algorithm where the bucket refills at a constant rate
40so|    of ``tokens_per_minute / 60`` tokens per second.  When the bucket does not
41eq|    have enough tokens, the caller blocks (or awaits) until refill provides the
42ip|    required amount.
43rd|
44zn|    Args:
45kr|        tokens_per_minute: Maximum number of call tokens allowed per minute.
46nf|            One call typically consumes one token.
47gs|        key: A human-readable identifier for this limiter, used in logs.
48th|
49ow|    Example:
50nr|        >>> limiter = TokenBucketRateLimiter(tokens_per_minute=30, key="gpt4")
51op|        >>> limiter.acquire()  # may block if bucket is empty
52ol|        >>> limiter.acquire(tokens=2)
53qo|    """
54th|
55or|    def __init__(self, tokens_per_minute: int, key: str = "global") -> None:
56ag|        self._tpm: int = tokens_per_minute
57iy|        self._key: str = key
58vz|        self._tokens: float = float(tokens_per_minute)
59eh|        self._last_refill: float = time.monotonic()
60an|        self._lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()
61te|        self._async_lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()
62nd|
63qd|    def _refill(self) -> None:
64ys|        """Refill the token bucket based on elapsed time since last refill.
65th|
66pw|        Adds ``elapsed_seconds * (tokens_per_minute / 60)`` tokens to the
67io|        bucket, capping at ``tokens_per_minute``.  Must be called while
68bc|        holding ``self._lock``.
69tu|        """
70cb|        now = time.monotonic()
71ak|        elapsed = now - self._last_refill
72ez|        self._last_refill = now
73rd|        refill_amount = elapsed * (self._tpm / 60.0)
74qb|        self._tokens = min(self._tokens + refill_amount, float(self._tpm))
75th|
76pl|    def acquire(self, tokens: int = 1) -> None:
77mx|        """Block until the requested number of tokens are available.
78th|
79ed|        Refills the bucket based on elapsed time, then waits if the bucket
80gw|        still does not have enough tokens.
81st|
82oz|        Args:
83ll|            tokens: Number of tokens to consume.  Usually 1 per LLM call.
84th|
85sn|        Example:
86yl|            >>> limiter = TokenBucketRateLimiter(tokens_per_minute=60)
87ug|            >>> limiter.acquire()
88tx|        """
89ii|        while True:
90tp|            with self._lock:
91kc|                self._refill()
92cx|                if self._tokens >= tokens:
93xx|                    self._tokens -= tokens
94zg|                    return
95hl|                # Calculate how long we must wait for the bucket to have enough
96dj|                deficit = tokens - self._tokens
97hz|                wait_time = deficit / (self._tpm / 60.0)
98th|
99qc|            # Sleep outside the lock so other threads can check/refill
100rs|            time.sleep(wait_time)
101st|
102gd|    async def aacquire(self, tokens: int = 1) -> None:
103do|        """Asynchronously wait until the requested number of tokens are available.
104th|
105qr|        Uses ``asyncio.sleep`` to yield control to the event loop while waiting,
106nz|        avoiding thread blocking in async contexts.
107th|
108oz|        Args:
109ll|            tokens: Number of tokens to consume.  Usually 1 per LLM call.
110th|
111sn|        Example:
112yl|            >>> limiter = TokenBucketRateLimiter(tokens_per_minute=60)
113qd|            >>> await limiter.aacquire()
114fa|        """
115ii|        while True:
116bl|            async with self._async_lock:
117qm|                # Use the thread-safe _refill since monotonic clocks are
118oa|                # process-global; the async lock prevents concurrent async writers.
119cp|                now = time.monotonic()
120dn|                elapsed = now - self._last_refill
121rh|                self._last_refill = now
122wm|                refill_amount = elapsed * (self._tpm / 60.0)
123nf|                self._tokens = min(self._tokens + refill_amount, float(self._tpm))
124th|
125cx|                if self._tokens >= tokens:
126xx|                    self._tokens -= tokens
127zg|                    return
128dj|                deficit = tokens - self._tokens
129hz|                wait_time = deficit / (self._tpm / 60.0)
130th|
131ha|            await asyncio.sleep(wait_time)
132nd|
133ff|    def wrap(self, llm: BaseLLMBackend) -> _RateLimitedLLM:
134ns|        """Wrap an LLM backend with this rate limiter.
135th|
136wm|        Returns a proxy that calls :meth:`acquire` before every synchronous
137fj|        invocation and :meth:`aacquire` before every async invocation.
138th|
139oz|        Args:
140lg|            llm: The backend to wrap.
141st|
142rt|        Returns:
143of|            A :class:`_RateLimitedLLM` proxy instance.
144th|
145sn|        Example:
146vo|            >>> limited = limiter.wrap(openai_backend)
147hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
148oq|            >>> response = limited.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
149dd|        """
150cu|        return _RateLimitedLLM(llm=llm, limiter=self)
151st|
152nd|
153oe|class _RateLimitedLLM(BaseLLMBackend):
154bt|    """Proxy LLM that enforces rate limiting on every call.
155th|
156nw|    All :class:`BaseLLMBackend` abstract methods are implemented.  Sync methods
157eo|    call :meth:`TokenBucketRateLimiter.acquire` before delegating; async
158kb|    methods call :meth:`TokenBucketRateLimiter.aacquire`.
159th|
160oo|    Streaming methods also acquire a token before yielding the first chunk.
161st|
162zn|    Args:
163td|        llm: The underlying :class:`BaseLLMBackend` to delegate to.
164uz|        limiter: The :class:`TokenBucketRateLimiter` to enforce.
165th|
166ow|    Example:
167wa|        >>> limited = _RateLimitedLLM(llm=openai_backend, limiter=limiter)
168dy|        >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
169ly|        >>> response = limited.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
170wd|    """
171st|
172gp|    def __init__(self, llm: BaseLLMBackend, limiter: TokenBucketRateLimiter) -> None:
173fu|        self._llm: BaseLLMBackend = llm
174pi|        self._limiter: TokenBucketRateLimiter = limiter
175th|
176jq|    @property
177tn|    def model_name(self) -> str:
178iz|        """The model name of the wrapped backend.
179th|
180rt|        Returns:
181bo|            The delegated backend's model name string.
182rq|        """
183lu|        return self._llm.model_name
184th|
185jq|    @property
186fb|    def provider(self) -> str:
187ok|        """The provider name of the wrapped backend.
188th|
189rt|        Returns:
190jg|            The delegated backend's provider string.
191xh|        """
192po|        return self._llm.provider
193rd|
194bk|    def invoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
195vi|        """Acquire a rate limit token then invoke the wrapped backend synchronously.
196th|
197oz|        Args:
198tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
199tz|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the wrapped backend.
200th|
201rt|        Returns:
202jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
203rd|
204nu|        Raises:
205rr|            BackendError: If the underlying backend's invoke fails.
206th|
207sn|        Example:
208hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
209xl|            >>> limited.invoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
210kj|        """
211dg|        self._limiter.acquire()
212vh|        return self._llm.invoke(messages, **kwargs)
213th|
214py|    async def ainvoke(self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any) -> AIMessage:
215yu|        """Acquire a rate limit token then invoke the wrapped backend asynchronously.
216th|
217oz|        Args:
218tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
219tz|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the wrapped backend.
220th|
221rt|        Returns:
222jj|            The model's reply as an :class:`AIMessage`.
223rd|
224nu|        Raises:
225zd|            BackendError: If the underlying backend's ainvoke fails.
226th|
227sn|        Example:
228hq|            >>> from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
229qq|            >>> await limited.ainvoke([HumanMessage(content="Hello")])
230qw|        """
231ht|        await self._limiter.aacquire()
232im|        return await self._llm.ainvoke(messages, **kwargs)
233rd|
234kh|    def stream(
235dp|        self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any
236iz|    ) -> Iterator[AIMessageChunk]:
237pb|        """Acquire a rate limit token then stream chunks synchronously.
238th|
239yl|        A single token is consumed before the first chunk is yielded.
240th|
241oz|        Args:
242tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
243tz|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the wrapped backend.
244th|
245qm|        Yields:
246mm|            :class:`AIMessageChunk` objects as they arrive.
247th|
248nu|        Raises:
249ao|            BackendError: If the underlying backend's stream fails.
250th|
251sn|        Example:
252er|            >>> for chunk in limited.stream([HumanMessage(content="Hi")]):
253ub|            ...     print(chunk.content, end="")
254mb|        """
255dg|        self._limiter.acquire()
256op|        yield from self._llm.stream(messages, **kwargs)
257th|
258de|    async def astream(
259dp|        self, messages: list[BaseMessage], **kwargs: Any
260uw|    ) -> AsyncIterator[AIMessageChunk]:
261cj|        """Acquire a rate limit token then stream chunks asynchronously.
262nd|
263yl|        A single token is consumed before the first chunk is yielded.
264th|
265oz|        Args:
266tt|            messages: Ordered list of conversation messages.
267tz|            **kwargs: Additional parameters forwarded to the wrapped backend.
268th|
269qm|        Yields:
270mm|            :class:`AIMessageChunk` objects as they arrive.
271st|
272nu|        Raises:
273lz|            BackendError: If the underlying backend's astream fails.
274th|
275sn|        Example:
276zg|            >>> async for chunk in limited.astream([HumanMessage(content="Hi")]):
277ub|            ...     print(chunk.content, end="")
278mq|        """
279ht|        await self._limiter.aacquire()
280og|        async for chunk in self._llm.astream(messages, **kwargs):
281ag|            yield chunk
282nd|
283pu|    def with_structured_output(self, schema: type[BaseModel]) -> _RateLimitedLLM:
284tb|        """Return a rate-limited backend configured for structured output.
285th|
286ay|        Delegates ``with_structured_output`` to the wrapped backend and wraps
287uv|        the result with this limiter.
288th|
289oz|        Args:
290fe|            schema: A Pydantic model class describing the desired output shape.
291st|
292rt|        Returns:
293zp|            A new :class:`_RateLimitedLLM` wrapping the structured-output backend.
294th|
295sn|        Example:
296te|            >>> from pydantic import BaseModel
297tt|            >>> class Answer(BaseModel):
298hp|            ...     text: str
299eo|            >>> structured = limited.with_structured_output(Answer)
300su|        """
301em|        structured_llm = self._llm.with_structured_output(schema)
302fu|        return _RateLimitedLLM(llm=structured_llm, limiter=self._limiter)
